Tutorial video: https://www.syncfusion.com/tutorial-videos

Download the example from GitHub: https://github.com/SyncfusionExamples/customize-the-appearance-of-the-react-treeview-using-templates
item.author: techguy

item.title: 创建数据库的用户界面：显示数据 | Creating a User Interface to the Database: Displaying the data
item.description:
在上一篇文章中，我们设置了一个小脚本来启动数据库和服务器，并允许我们通过命令行访问特定记录。

现在让我们将其扩展为所有连接项目的递归视图并使其可点击。 最后，它看起来像这样：

访问数据

正如我们在概念证明示例中已经看到的那样，我们可以通过(This)和:函数访问当前记录。 我们可以使用它来定义树的标题：“XY 的家谱（DD-MM-YYYY 到 MM-DD-YYYY）”。 如果没有可用的年份，让我们打印<unknown date> 。

所以我们混合了输入参数，主要是字符串和数据库值。 最重要的是，我们有一些通常保留的字符，例如< in <unknown date> 。 我们该如何处理呢？

出于这些目的，我们可以使用已编译的hl.so库中的函数，它提供了比未编译的标准 lisp 函数更快的格式化函数。 如果您正在处理用户输入，则永远不要使用标准的prin函数，因为它为跨端脚本攻击打开了所有大门！ 让我们将其作为标题传递给我们的页面。 通过一点引导，我们得到了这个：

接下来，让我们在列表的一行中打印出此人和他/她的伴侣（如果有的话），并在随后的缩进行中打印出他们的孩子。 它应该如下所示：

  • 我们可以通过开始一个低序列表<ul>来做到这一点。 第一个列表项<li>非常简单：

     (<ul> NIL (<li> NIL (ht:Prin (: nm)) (when (: mate) (prin " -- ") (ht:Prin (: mate nm)) ) )

    注意：如果我们怀疑里面有一些保留字符，我们只需要ht:Prin 。 为了安全起见，让我们为所有数据库记录保留它。

    接下来，让我们打印这个人的孩子。 为了创建缩进，我们需要将它们放在当前列表中的另一个无序列表<ul>中。 所以我们定义另一个<li>并使用(: kids)获取孩子列表。 然后我们遍历列表并将它们中的每一个打印在新行上。

     (<li> NIL (when (: kids) (<ul> NIL (for This @ (<li> NIL (ht:Prin (: nm))) ) ) ) )

    在一些 Bootstrap 类的帮助下，它是这样的：

    使其递归

    显然，使用这种方法，我们必须手动指定每一层。 如果我们想添加孙子，我们需要编写另一个循环，依此类推。 所以这是匿名递归的完美案例。

    我们可以使用recur / recurse函数定义匿名递归：

    (recur fun) -> any

    (recurse ..) -> any

    通过动态定义函数recurse来实现匿名递归。 在fun执行期间，符号recurse绑定到函数定义fun 。

递归级别应该在我们打开第一个<ul>标记后立即开始，并为每个孩子执行。 唯一的论点是( This ) 。

因此，我们使用mapc递归 ( (: kids) kids) 的每个列表元素，而不是(<ul> NIL (for (: kids) )：

 (<ul> NIL (mapc recurse (: kids)))

这是完整的打印输出功能，包括引导类：

 (<ul> "list-group list-group-flush" (recur (This) (<li> "list-group-item " (ht:Prin (: nm)) (when (: mate) (prin " -- ") (ht:Prin (: mate nm)) ) ) (when (: kids) (<ul> NIL (mapc recurse (: kids))) ) ) )

现在我们递归地得到输出：

创建链接

好吧，您可能期望的下一件事是您可以单击每个名称以在其自己的页面中展开它们。 可以使用<href>函数创建链接，语法：

 ( <href> #{displayed text} #{relative or absolute path} )

在这种情况下，我们只需要一个相对路径，因为基本 URL 将保持不变。 通常，任何路径参数都会在问号?之后附加到基本 URL。 为了按照我们需要的方式对我们的数据库对象进行编码，我们可以使用 ht 库中的另一个函数ht:Fmt 。 因此，我们显示一个链接而不是名称：

 (<href> (: nm) (pack "?" (ht:Fmt This)))

对于合作伙伴：

 (<href> (: mate nm) (pack "?" (ht:Fmt (: mate))))

就这样！ 现在我们已经在数据库项目上创建了一个可点击的递归视图。

在下一篇文章中，我们将看到如何从可以通过 REST API 共享的这些数据中生成 JSON 列表。

  • 罗德尔·E·达古曼潘 - Feb 22 2月22日



原文:

In the last post, we have set up a small script that starts the database and the server and allows us access to a specific record via the command line.

Now let's expand this to a recursive view on all connected items and make it clickable. In the end, it will look like this:

Accessing the data

As we could see already in our proof of concept-example, we can access the current record via (This) and the : functions. We could use this to define the headline of our tree: "Family tree of X Y (DD-MM-YYYY to MM-DD-YYYY)". In case there is no year available, let's print <unknown date>.

So we have a mix of input parameters, mostly strings and database values. On top, we have some characters that are usually reserved, such as < in <unknown date>. How can we handle this?

For these kind of purposes, we can use the functions of the compiled hl.so library which provides formatting functions that are faster than the uncompiled standard lisp functions. ht:Prin replaces reserved characters by their HTML-counterpart such as <. If you're dealing with user input, you should never use the standard prin functions, because it leaves all doors open for cross-side scripting attacks! (We will come back to that point in a later post!)

(ht:Prin
   "Family tree of "
   (: nm) 
   " ("
   (or (datStr (: dat)) "<unknown date>")
   " to "
   (or (datStr (: fin)) "<unknown date>" )
   ") " ) )

Let's pass this as title to our page. With a little bit of bootstrap, we get this:

Next, let's print out the person and his/her mate (if available) in one line of the list, and their kids on subsequent, indented lines. It should look like this:

  • Person A -- Partner (optional)
    • Child 1 of A
    • Child 2 of A
    • ...

We can do this by starting an underordered list <ul>. The first list item <li> is pretty straightforward:

(<ul> NIL
   (<li> NIL 
      (ht:Prin (: nm))
      (when (: mate)
         (prin " -- ")
         (ht:Prin (: mate nm)) ) )

Note: we only need ht:Prin if we suspect some reserved characters inside. Let's keep it for all database records, just to be on the safe side.

Next, let's print the person's kids. In order to create the indentation, we need to put them in another unordered list <ul> within the current one. So we define another <li> and get the list of kids with (: kids). Then we loop through the list and print each of them on a new line.

   (<li> NIL
      (when (: kids)
         (<ul> NIL (for This @
            (<li> NIL (ht:Prin (: nm))) ) ) ) )

@ is the result of the last evaluation, in this case the current kid.

With help of some Bootstrap classes, this is how it looks like:

Making it recursive

Obviously, with this approach we have to specify each layer manually. If we want to add the grandchildren, we need to write another loop, and so on. So this is a perfect case for anonymous recursion.

We can define an anonymous recursion using the recur/recurse functions:

(recur fun) -> any

(recurse ..) -> any

Implements anonymous recursion, by defining the function recurse on the fly. During the execution of fun, the symbol recurse is bound to the function definition fun.

The recursion level should just start right after we opened the first <ul> tag and be executed for each child. The only argument is ( This ).

So, instead of (<ul> NIL (for (: kids), we recurse each list element of (: kids) using mapc:

 (<ul> NIL (mapc recurse (: kids)))

This is the full printout function including the bootstrap classes:

(<ul> "list-group list-group-flush"
   (recur (This)
      (<li> "list-group-item "
         (ht:Prin (: nm))
         (when (: mate)
            (prin " -- ")
            (ht:Prin (: mate nm)) ) )
      (when (: kids)
         (<ul> NIL (mapc recurse (: kids))) ) ) )

Now we get the output recursively:

Creating Links

Well, the next thing you would probably expect is that you can click on each name to expand them in their own page. Links can be created using the <href> function, syntax:

( <href>  #{displayed text} #{relative or absolute path} )

In this case, we only need a relative path, because the base-URL will stay the same. In general, any path arguments are appended to the base-URL after a question mark ?. In order to encode our database object the way we need it, we can use another function from the ht-library, ht:Fmt. So instead of the name, we display a link:

(<href> (: nm) (pack "?" (ht:Fmt This)))

and for the partner:

(<href> (: mate nm) (pack "?" (ht:Fmt (: mate))))

And that's all! Now we've created a clickable, recursive view on the database items.

In the next post, we will see how we can generate a JSON list out of this data that can be shared via a REST API.

  • https://software-lab.de/doc/index.html
  • https://software-lab.de/doc/app.html

Maiqui Tomé 🇧🇷 - Mar 27

Rodel E. Dagumampan - Feb 22
item.author: miatemma

item.title: 在 Python 的特定会议中抓取 Google Scholar 论文 | Scrape Google Scholar Papers within a particular conference in Python
item.description:
过滤的工作原理

要过滤结果，您需要使用source:运算符，它将搜索结果限制为由名称中包含"NIPS"的来源发布的文档。

除了OR运算符，也可以使用此运算符，即source:NIPS OR source:"Neural Information" 。 因此搜索查询将变为：

 search terms source:NIPS OR source:"Neural Information"

先决条件

使用 CSS 选择器抓取基础知识

如果您还没有使用 CSS 选择器进行抓取，我有一篇专门的博客文章介绍了如何在网络抓取时使用 CSS 选择器，其中涵盖了它是什么、利弊，以及为什么从网络抓取的角度来看它们很重要和展示在网页抓取时使用 CSS 选择器的最常见方法。

如果它将是一个项目，则单独的虚拟环境

简而言之，它创建了一组独立的已安装库，包括可以在同一系统中相互共存的不同 Python 版本，从而防止库或 Python 版本冲突。

如果您之前没有使用过虚拟环境，请查看使用 Virtualenv 和 Poetry 博客文章的专用 Python 虚拟环境教程以熟悉一下。

安装库：

 pip install requests parsel

减少被阻止的机会

请求可能会被阻止。 看看如何减少网页抓取时被阻止的机会，有十一种方法可以绕过大多数网站的阻止。

完整代码

from parsel import Selector import requests , json , os def scrape_conference_publications ( query : str , source : list [ str ]): if source : # source:NIPS OR source:Neural Information sources = " OR " . join ([ f 'source: { item } ' for item in source ]) # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls params = { "q" : f ' { query . lower () } { sources } ' , # search query "hl" : "en" , # language of the search "gl" : "us" # country of the search } # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#custom-headers headers = { "User-Agent" : "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36" } html = requests . get ( "https://scholar.google.com/scholar" , params = params , headers = headers , timeout = 30 ) selector = Selector ( html . text ) publications = [] for result in selector . css ( ".gs_r.gs_scl" ): title = result . css ( ".gs_rt" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () link = result . css ( ".gs_rt a::attr(href)" ). get () result_id = result . attrib [ "data-cid" ] snippet = result . css ( ".gs_rs::text" ). get () publication_info = result . css ( ".gs_a" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () cite_by_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( ".gs_or_btn.gs_nph+ a::attr(href)" ). get () } ' all_versions_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( "a~ a+ .gs_nph::attr(href)" ). get () } ' related_articles_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( "a:nth-child(4)::attr(href)" ). get () } ' pdf_file_title = result . css ( ".gs_or_ggsm a" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () pdf_file_link = result . css ( ".gs_or_ggsm a::attr(href)" ). get () publications . append ({ "result_id" : result_id , "title" : title , "link" : link , "snippet" : snippet , "publication_info" : publication_info , "cite_by_link" : cite_by_link , "all_versions_link" : all_versions_link , "related_articles_link" : related_articles_link , "pdf" : { "title" : pdf_file_title , "link" : pdf_file_link } }) # or return publications instead # return publications print ( json . dumps ( publications , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False )) scrape_conference_publications ( query = "anatomy" , source = [ "NIPS" , "Neural Information" ])

代码说明

定义一个函数：

 def scrape_conference_publications ( query : str , source : list [ str ]): # further code...
代码解释
query: str 告诉 Python query参数应该是一个string 。
source: list[str] 告诉 Python source参数应该是一个strings list 。

检查源是否可用并转换接收到的source参数：

 if source : # iterates via list comprehension over recieved list of sources (strings) # and joins() them with " OR " sources = " OR " . join ([ f 'source: { item } ' for item in source ]) # becomes: source:NIPS OR source:Neural Information, which can be used in a search query

创建 URL 参数、 user-agent并将它们传递给请求：

 # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls params = { "q" : f ' { query . lower () } { sources } ' , # search query "hl" : "en" , # language of the search "gl" : "us" # country of the search } # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#custom-headers headers = { "User-Agent" : "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36" } html = requests . get ( "https://scholar.google.com/scholar" , params = params , headers = headers , timeout = 30 ) selector = Selector ( html . text )

创建一个临时list来存储数据，并迭代有机结果：

 publications = [] for result in selector . css ( ".gs_r.gs_scl" ): title = result . css ( ".gs_rt" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () link = result . css ( ".gs_rt a::attr(href)" ). get () result_id = result . attrib [ "data-cid" ] snippet = result . css ( ".gs_rs::text" ). get () publication_info = result . css ( ".gs_a" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () cite_by_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( ".gs_or_btn.gs_nph+ a::attr(href)" ). get () } ' all_versions_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( "a~ a+ .gs_nph::attr(href)" ). get () } ' related_articles_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( "a:nth-child(4)::attr(href)" ). get () } ' pdf_file_title = result . css ( ".gs_or_ggsm a" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () pdf_file_link = result . css ( ".gs_or_ggsm a::attr(href)" ). get ()

将结果作为字典附加到临时列表中，返回或打印提取的数据：

 publications . append ({ "result_id" : result_id , "title" : title , "link" : link , "snippet" : snippet , "publication_info" : publication_info , "cite_by_link" : cite_by_link , "all_versions_link" : all_versions_link , "related_articles_link" : related_articles_link , "pdf" : { "title" : pdf_file_title , "link" : pdf_file_link } }) # return publications print ( json . dumps ( publications , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False )) scrape_conference_publications ( query = "anatomy" , source = [ "NIPS" , "Neural Information" ])

输出：

 [ { "result_id" : "hjgaRkq_oOEJ" , "title" : "Differential representation of arm movement direction in relation to cortical anatomy and function" , "link" : "https://iopscience.iop.org/article/10.1088/1741-2560/6/1/016006/meta" , "snippet" : "… " , "publication_info" : "T Ball, A Schulze-Bonhage, A Aertsen… - Journal of neural …, 2009 - iopscience.iop.org" , "cite_by_link" : "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?cites=16258204980532099206&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en" , "all_versions_link" : "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?cluster=16258204980532099206&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5" , "related_articles_link" : "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?q=related:hjgaRkq_oOEJ:scholar.google.com/&scioq=anatomy+source:NIPS+OR+source:Neural+Information&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5" , "pdf" : { "title" : "[PDF] psu.edu" , "link" : "http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/download?doi=10.1.1.324.1523&rep=rep1&type=pdf" } }, ... other results
]

或者，您可以使用来自 SerpApi 的Google Scholar Organic Results API来实现它。

最大的区别是您不需要从头开始创建解析器、维护它、弄清楚如何扩展它，最重要的是，如何绕过 Google 的块，从而弄清楚如何设置代理和 CAPTCHA 解决方案。

 # pip install google-search-results import os , json from serpapi import GoogleSearch from urllib.parse import urlsplit , parse_qsl params = { # https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.getenv "api_key" : os . getenv ( "API_KEY" ), # your serpapi API key "engine" : "google_scholar" , # search engine "q" : "AI source:NIPS" , # search query "hl" : "en" , # language # "as_ylo": "2017", # from 2017 # "as_yhi": "2021", # to 2021 "start" : "0" # first page } search = GoogleSearch ( params ) publications = [] publications_is_present = True while publications_is_present : results = search . get_dict () print ( f "Currently extracting page # { results . get ( 'serpapi_pagination' , {} ). get ( 'current' ) } .." ) for result in results [ "organic_results" ]: position = result [ "position" ] title = result [ "title" ] publication_info_summary = result [ "publication_info" ][ "summary" ] result_id = result [ "result_id" ] link = result . get ( "link" ) result_type = result . get ( "type" ) snippet = result . get ( "snippet" ) publications . append ({ "page_number" : results . get ( "serpapi_pagination" , {}). get ( "current" ), "position" : position + 1 , "result_type" : result_type , "title" : title , "link" : link , "result_id" : result_id , "publication_info_summary" : publication_info_summary , "snippet" : snippet , }) if "next" in results . get ( "serpapi_pagination" , {}): # splits URL in parts as a dict and passes it to a GoogleSearch() class. search . params_dict . update ( dict ( parse_qsl ( urlsplit ( results [ "serpapi_pagination" ][ "next" ]). query ))) else : papers_is_present = False print ( json . dumps ( organic_results_data , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False ))
  • 在推特上加入我们 | 添加功能请求💫或错误🐞

Dominika Zając (she/her) 🇵🇱 - Dominika Zając (她/她) 🇵🇱 - Mar 24 3月24日



原文:

How filtering works

To filter results, you need to use source: operator which restricts search results to documents published by sources containing "NIPS" in their name.

This operator can be used in addition to OR operator i.e source:NIPS OR source:"Neural Information". So the search query would become:

search terms source:NIPS OR source:"Neural Information"

Prerequisites

Basic knowledge scraping with CSS selectors

CSS selectors declare which part of the markup a style applies to thus allowing to extract data from matching tags and attributes.

If you haven't scraped with CSS selectors, there's a dedicated blog post of mine about how to use CSS selectors when web-scraping that covers what it is, pros and cons, and why they're matter from a web-scraping perspective and show the most common approaches of using CSS selectors when web scraping.

Separate virtual environment if it will be a project

In short, it's a thing that creates an independent set of installed libraries including different Python versions that can coexist with each other in the same system thus preventing libraries or Python version conflicts.

If you didn't work with a virtual environment before, have a look at the dedicated Python virtual environments tutorial using Virtualenv and Poetry blog post of mine to get familiar.

📌Note: this is not a strict requirement for this blog post.

Install libraries:

pip install requests parsel

Reduce the chance of being blocked

There's a chance that a request might be blocked. Have a look at how to reduce the chance of being blocked while web-scraping, there are eleven methods to bypass blocks from most websites.

Full Code

from parsel import Selector
import requests, json, os def scrape_conference_publications(query: str, source: list[str]): if source: # source:NIPS OR source:Neural Information
 sources = " OR ".join([f'source:{item}' for item in source]) # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls
 params = { "q": f'{query.lower()} {sources}', # search query
 "hl": "en", # language of the search
 "gl": "us" # country of the search
 } # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#custom-headers
 headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36" } html = requests.get("https://scholar.google.com/scholar", params=params, headers=headers, timeout=30) selector = Selector(html.text) publications = [] for result in selector.css(".gs_r.gs_scl"): title = result.css(".gs_rt").xpath("normalize-space()").get() link = result.css(".gs_rt a::attr(href)").get() result_id = result.attrib["data-cid"] snippet = result.css(".gs_rs::text").get() publication_info = result.css(".gs_a").xpath("normalize-space()").get() cite_by_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css(".gs_or_btn.gs_nph+ a::attr(href)").get()}' all_versions_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css("a~ a+ .gs_nph::attr(href)").get()}' related_articles_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css("a:nth-child(4)::attr(href)").get()}' pdf_file_title = result.css(".gs_or_ggsm a").xpath("normalize-space()").get() pdf_file_link = result.css(".gs_or_ggsm a::attr(href)").get() publications.append({ "result_id": result_id, "title": title, "link": link, "snippet": snippet, "publication_info": publication_info, "cite_by_link": cite_by_link, "all_versions_link": all_versions_link, "related_articles_link": related_articles_link, "pdf": { "title": pdf_file_title, "link": pdf_file_link } }) # or return publications instead
 # return publications
 print(json.dumps(publications, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)) scrape_conference_publications(query="anatomy", source=["NIPS", "Neural Information"])

Code explanation

Define a function:

def scrape_conference_publications(query: str, source: list[str]): # further code...
Code Explanation
query: str tells Python that query argument should be a string.
source: list[str] tells Python that source argument should be a list of strings.

Check if the source is available and transform the received source argument:

if source: # iterates via list comprehension over recieved list of sources (strings)
 # and joins() them with " OR "
 sources = " OR ".join([f'source:{item}' for item in source]) # becomes: source:NIPS OR source:Neural Information, which can be used in a search query

Create URL parameters, user-agent and pass them to a request:

# https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls
params = { "q": f'{query.lower()} {sources}', # search query
 "hl": "en", # language of the search
 "gl": "us" # country of the search
} # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#custom-headers
headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36"
} html = requests.get("https://scholar.google.com/scholar", params=params, headers=headers, timeout=30)
selector = Selector(html.text)

Create a temporary list to store the data, and iterate over organic results:

publications = [] for result in selector.css(".gs_r.gs_scl"): title = result.css(".gs_rt").xpath("normalize-space()").get() link = result.css(".gs_rt a::attr(href)").get() result_id = result.attrib["data-cid"] snippet = result.css(".gs_rs::text").get() publication_info = result.css(".gs_a").xpath("normalize-space()").get() cite_by_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css(".gs_or_btn.gs_nph+ a::attr(href)").get()}' all_versions_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css("a~ a+ .gs_nph::attr(href)").get()}' related_articles_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css("a:nth-child(4)::attr(href)").get()}' pdf_file_title = result.css(".gs_or_ggsm a").xpath("normalize-space()").get() pdf_file_link = result.css(".gs_or_ggsm a::attr(href)").get()

Append the results as a dictionary to a temporary list, return or print extracted data:

publications.append({ "result_id": result_id, "title": title, "link": link, "snippet": snippet, "publication_info": publication_info, "cite_by_link": cite_by_link, "all_versions_link": all_versions_link, "related_articles_link": related_articles_link, "pdf": { "title": pdf_file_title, "link": pdf_file_link } }) # return publications

print(json.dumps(publications, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)) scrape_conference_publications(query="anatomy", source=["NIPS", "Neural Information"])

Outputs:

[ { "result_id": "hjgaRkq_oOEJ", "title": "Differential representation of arm movement direction in relation to cortical anatomy and function", "link": "https://iopscience.iop.org/article/10.1088/1741-2560/6/1/016006/meta", "snippet": "… ", "publication_info": "T Ball, A Schulze-Bonhage, A Aertsen… - Journal of neural …, 2009 - iopscience.iop.org", "cite_by_link": "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?cites=16258204980532099206&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en", "all_versions_link": "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?cluster=16258204980532099206&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5", "related_articles_link": "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?q=related:hjgaRkq_oOEJ:scholar.google.com/&scioq=anatomy+source:NIPS+OR+source:Neural+Information&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5", "pdf": { "title": "[PDF] psu.edu", "link": "http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/download?doi=10.1.1.324.1523&rep=rep1&type=pdf" } }, ... other results
]

Alternatively, you can achieve it using Google Scholar Organic Results API from SerpApi.

The biggest difference is that you don't need to create a parser from scratch, maintain it, figure out how to scale it, and most importantly, how to bypass blocks from Google thus figuring out how to set up proxies and CAPTCHA solving solutions.

# pip install google-search-results

import os, json
from serpapi import GoogleSearch
from urllib.parse import urlsplit, parse_qsl params = { # https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.getenv
 "api_key": os.getenv("API_KEY"), # your serpapi API key
 "engine": "google_scholar", # search engine
 "q": "AI source:NIPS", # search query
 "hl": "en", # language
 # "as_ylo": "2017", # from 2017
 # "as_yhi": "2021", # to 2021
 "start": "0" # first page
 } search = GoogleSearch(params) publications = [] publications_is_present = True
while publications_is_present: results = search.get_dict() print(f"Currently extracting page #{results.get('serpapi_pagination', {}).get('current')}..") for result in results["organic_results"]: position = result["position"] title = result["title"] publication_info_summary = result["publication_info"]["summary"] result_id = result["result_id"] link = result.get("link") result_type = result.get("type") snippet = result.get("snippet") publications.append({ "page_number": results.get("serpapi_pagination", {}).get("current"), "position": position + 1, "result_type": result_type, "title": title, "link": link, "result_id": result_id, "publication_info_summary": publication_info_summary, "snippet": snippet, }) if "next" in results.get("serpapi_pagination", {}): # splits URL in parts as a dict and passes it to a GoogleSearch() class.
 search.params_dict.update(dict(parse_qsl(urlsplit(results["serpapi_pagination"]["next"]).query))) else: papers_is_present = False print(json.dumps(organic_results_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
  • Code in the onlie IDE
  • SerpApi Google Scholar Organic Results API

Join us on Twitter | YouTube

Add a Feature Request💫 or a Bug🐞

Dominika Zając (she/her) 🇵🇱 - Mar 24

item.title: 市场整合：连接亚马逊、eBay、沃尔玛和 Etsy | Marketplace Integration: Connect with Amazon, eBay, Walmart and Etsy
item.description:
市场集成的开发是营销自动化、运输管理、WMS、订单、库存软件提供商或任何其他电子零售商 B2B 解决方案的必备条件。

它允许此类电子商务软件管理从市场上列出的商店中检索到的与客户、订单、产品、类别等相关的数据。

最受欢迎的市场是亚马逊、eBay、沃尔玛和 Etsy。 与所有这些集成允许软件提供商增加其潜在客户的数量，访问所有必要的数据，并扩展他们的软件功能。

使用 API2Cart API 方法，您将能够添加、同步和删除与从 Amazon、eBay、Etsy 和 Walmart 上列出的商店收到的产品、订单、类别相关的数据。

您可以在此处找到详细信息： https ://rb.gy/wr1rdn



原文:

The development of marketplace integration is a must-have for marketing automation, shipping management, WMS, order, inventory software provider, or any other B2B solution for e-retailers.

It allows such eCommerce software to manage the data connected with customers, orders, products, categories, etc. retrieved from the stores listed on marketplaces.

The most popular marketplaces are Amazon, eBay, Walmart, and Etsy. The integration with all of them allows software providers to increase their prospective customers' number, access all the necessary data, and expand their software functionality.

API2Cart provides a unified API that allows you to get connected with multiple marketplaces at once.

With API2Cart API methods, you’ll be able to add, sync, and delete the data related to the products, orders, categories received from stores listed on Amazon, eBay, Etsy, and Walmart.

The detailed info you can find here: https://rb.gy/wr1rdn

item.title: Array.reverse() - 用于反转数组 | Array.reverse() - for reversing an array
item.description:
狄利安·梅吉达

发表于本文是阵列方法系列的第九篇。 在本文中，我将解释reverse数组方法。

什么是反向方法？

我认为这个名字说明了一切😅。 数组的reverse方法将数组反转，使得数组中的最后一项成为第一项，第一项成为最后一项。

此方法会修改数组，因此我建议在反转之前克隆以避免应用程序中的突变冲突。

反向方法的语法

虽然此方法会改变数组，但它也会返回反转的数组。

没有反向方法

这是一个模仿reverse方法的示例：

 const array = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 , 6 , 7 , 8 ] const reversed = [] for ( let i = array . length - 1 ; i > - 1 ; i -- ) { const item = array [ i ] reversed . push ( item ) } console . log ( reversed ) // [8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1]

这从后到前循环遍历数组中的每个项目。 虽然这个循环不会改变原始数组，但它类似于reverse在后台所做的。

使用反向方法

以下是使用reverse实现先前结果的方法：

 const array = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 , 6 , 7 , 8 ] const reversed = array . reverse () console . log ( reversed ) // [8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1] console . log ( array ) // initial array is also modified // [8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1]

如您所见，初始数组也被修改了。 因此，为避免突变冲突，您可以像这样克隆数组：

 const array = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 , 6 , 7 , 8 ] const reversed = [... array ]. reverse () console . log ( reversed ) // [8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1] console . log ( array ) // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]

这样，初始数组保持不变，您也可以获得反转数组。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

Dillion Megida

Posted on Apr 29

This article is the ninth of the Array Method Series. In this article, I will explain the reverse Array method.

What is the Reverse Method?

I think the name says it all 😅. The reverse method of arrays reverses the array such that the last item in the array becomes the first, and the first item becomes the last.

This method modifies the array, so I recommend cloning before reversing to avoid mutation conflicts in your applications.

Syntax of the Reverse Method

While this method mutates the array, it also returns the reversed array.

Without the Reverse Method

The reverse method is an abstracted function that flips the array from one side to the other. Here's an example imitating the reverse method:

const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8] const reversed = [] for (let i = array.length - 1; i > -1; i--) { const item = array[i] reversed.push(item)
} console.log(reversed)
// [8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1]

This loops through each item in the array from the back to the front. Although this loop does not mutate the original array, it is similar to what reverse does in the background.

With the Reverse Method

Here's how you achieve the previous result with reverse:

const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8] const reversed = array.reverse() console.log(reversed)
// [8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1] console.log(array)
// initial array is also modified
// [8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1]

As you can see, the initial array is also modified. So to avoid mutation conflicts, you can clone the array like this:

const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8] const reversed = [...array].reverse() console.log(reversed)
// [8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1] console.log(array)
// [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]

With this, the initial array stays the same, and you can get the reversed array also.

Cristian Fernando - Apr 27
item.author: dillionmegida

item.title: 使用调试模式对 API 进行故障排除 | Troubleshoot your APIs with Debug Mode
item.description:
如果您经常使用 API，您将了解确保它们受到保护、保护和管理以及构建核心功能的困难。

如果您还没有，您需要查看 API 管理。 在本质上，

使用开源Gravitee.io API 管理平台（GitHub 存储库） ，您可以创建、设计和管理 API 的生命周期。 借助许多丰富而强大的策略，您可以使用大量工具和技术来为您的 API 提供强大的功能，例如：

  • 检查身份验证并管理其他安全方面
  • 转换标头
  • 协议调解
  • 保护您的后端免受恶意和意外流量高峰的影响

但有时，您的设计变得如此复杂，以至于几乎不可能知道设计和配置问题出现在哪里。 不要惊慌，调试模式可以帮助您。 调试模式是一个释放 API Publisher 力量的功能，允许您设计一个 API，（影子）部署它（别担心，我们稍后会解释影子部署），并准确了解哪些策略有已应用于 API 调用。

在这篇博文中，我们将设身处地为 API 发布者着想，研究一个显示一些奇怪行为的 API。 我们将通过一个简单的 API 示例，不仅向您展示如何使用调试模式，还向您展示它是如何工作的。

我是谁 ？

我是 Yann Tavernier，是出色的 APIM 团队的开发人员。 我有机会在调试模式下工作，我很高兴带您完成并解释此功能！

关键概念和组件

在我们开始了解调试模式之前，让我们概述一下我们将在本博客中使用的所有关键概念和组件。

关键部件：

  • 网关是 APIM 的组件，负责处理来自客户的请求。 除了充当 API 的代理之外，它还将对传入请求和传出响应应用转换、检查、速率限制等。

关键概念：

  • 计划是消费者应用程序 API 之上的访问和服务层。 消费者应用程序需要订阅计划才能使用 API。
  • 策略是可以对 API 请求或响应执行以对其执行检查、转换或其他服务的服务或操作。 除了开箱即用的策略外，您还可以创建自己的策略。

介绍我们的场景

我们现在将介绍我们的简单场景，以了解如何使用调试模式。

我们所有的示例都将在本地安装上完成。 您可以使用此命令通过 docker-compose 运行 APIM 3.17：
cd /tmp && mkdir -p apim && cd apim && curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gravitee-io/gravitee-docker/master/apim/3.x/docker-compose.yml -o "docker-compose.yml" && export APIM_VERSION=3.17.1 && docker-compose down -v && docker-compose pull && docker-compose up

要访问控制台，请转到以下地址： http://localhost:8084

默认凭据是：

  • 登录：管理员
  • 密码：admin

如果您对更详细的指南感兴趣，可以查看Ljubica Lazarevic 的这篇很棒的博客！

我们将依赖一个名为 Library backend 的 API。 由于它的 API 定义，您可以在此处导入它。

此后端可在http://localhost:8082/library-backend上访问并返回模拟数据集：

 { "books": [ { "title": "A tale of two cities", "author": "Charles Dickens", "_internalReference": "gio_b1" }, { "title": "The Lord of the Rings", "author": "JRR Tolkien", "_internalReference": "gio_b2" }, { "title": "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy", "author": "Douglas Adams", "_internalReference": "gio_b3" } ] }

我们稍后会分析这个数据集。

作为 API 发布者，我想设计一个“库”API 来访问我的库后端。
您可以按照文档创建它并按照本指南逐步操作，也可以直接导入生成的 API 定义。
首先，我需要选择用于我的 API 的计划类型。 我们将选择两个计划：一个称为 Free 的 Keyless 计划和一个称为 Premium 的 API-Key 计划。

让我们首先设计免费计划。 该计划允许任何人访问我们的 API。 为了保护我们的后端系统并提高性能，我们希望通过此计划管理将访问我们的 API 的流量。 由于速率限制政策，我们将限制访问。 需要注意的一点 - 因为这是一个免费计划，所以使用应用程序不需要创建订阅。

由于 API 将可公开访问，我们希望在响应中隐藏一些私有数据，即_internalReference字段。 为此，我们将使用JSON 到 JSON 转换策略。

这里是 JsonToJson Policy 的配置。 其目的是删除books数组中的_internalReference字段：

 [ { "operation": "remove", "spec": { "books": { "*": { "_internalReference": "" } } } } ]

让我们部署 API（您可以通过在出现提示时同步 API 来做到这一点）并使用 cURL 调用它：
调用curl http://localhost:8082/library会输出：

 { "books": [ { "title": "A tale of two cities", "author": "Charles Dickens" }, { "title": "The Lord of the Rings", "author": "JRR Tolkien" }, { "title": "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy", "author": "Douglas Adams" } ] }

我们已经成功隐藏了 API 暴露的敏感内部数据。 但是，我们的开发团队很可能需要访问这些数据以进行测试。 所以我们现在要做的是创建一个内部API 密钥计划，以允许对数据进行授权访问。它通过验证作为标头或查询参数传递的 API 密钥来工作，并链接到已订阅的应用程序到 API。 您可以在有关如何设置的文档中找到更多信息。

请记住， _internalReference删除是在免费增值计划中完成的。 在这里，使用内部计划，我们保留原始数据。 我们将利用这个机会使用分配属性策略在上下文中添加内部属性。 该属性将是应用程序 ID 的串联，后跟“-internal”，可通过“internal”键访问。

为此，我们将分配属性策略配置为添加具有以下值的internal属性： {#context.attributes["application"]}-internal 。

现在，使用curl http://locahost:8082/library?api-key=7cc755d1-378b-4099-9650-9ee9330be55c的 API 密钥调用 API 将输出：

 { "books": [ { "title": "A tale of two cities", "author": "Charles Dickens", "_internalReference": "gio_b1" }, { "title": "The Lord of the Rings", "author": "JRR Tolkien", "_internalReference": "gio_b2" }, { "title": "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy", "author": "Douglas Adams", "_internalReference": "gio_b3" } ] }

注意：您可能已经注意到我们看不到我们设置的属性。 实际上，该属性仅在调用时存在于 API 网关中。 这是我们正在使用的策略的一种行为，它在网关内部。

最后，我们将再次使用 JSON 到 JSON 的转换在 API 上创建一个流，以根据特定条件对图书应用折扣。

如果响应是以下任一情况，则将应用折扣：

  • 请求通过 Header - “X-Library-Discount”:“折扣百分比”完成
  • 属性“内部”不为空。

默认情况下，折扣值将是 X-Library-Discount 标头的值，或默认为 10%。

为此，我们将首先配置策略的条件：
{(#request.headeers['X-Library-Discount'] != null && #request.headers['X-Library-Discount'].size() > 0) || {#context.attributes['internal'] != null}}

然后，我们将为 Json to Json Policy 使用此配置：

 [ { "operation": "shift", "spec": { "*": "&" } }, { "operation": "default", "spec": { "discount": "{#request.headers['X-Library-Discount'] != null ? #request.headers['X-Library-Discount'][0] : 10}%" } } ]

使用curl http://localhost:8082/library?api-key=7cc755d1-378b-4099-9650-9ee9330be55c调用 API 将输出：

 { "message":"Request failed unintentionally", "http_status_code":500 }

我们的上一个流程存在一些问题，但发生了什么？ 仅使用 cURL 或 Postman 很难说。 随着 API 设计复杂性的增加，出现问题的风险也随之增加。 在解决所有未解决的问题之前，我们如何避免将 API 部署给用户？

调试模式来了

调试模式功能提供了一种对网关内部发生的情况进行全局了解和概览的方法。 有了这个功能，我们现在可以使用我们的 HTTP 客户端发送请求，并准确查看已执行的策略及其输入和输出。

在以下屏幕截图中，您可以看到使用调试模式检查的请求流示例：

该请求使用与之前相同的 API 密钥，我们可以看到响应以“500 - Internal Server Error”结尾。

让我们看看调试模式功能向我们展示了什么。 此功能的第一个重要元素是时间线。 它代表了每个运行的策略，形状像一张卡片。 一张卡片由几个元素组成：

  • 策略的名称（例如分配属性）
  • 它的阶段（或者：平台、安全、计划、api）和范围（或者：标题、正文）：计划>标题
  • 它的执行时间（例如0.227ms）

它代表了您的策略执行的真实顺序，这可能与您的初始设计有所不同。 您可能会注意到执行可能与您的预期不同！

如果您是 Gravitee 的普通用户，您可能习惯于配置策略并选择以下范围之一： REQUEST 、 REQUEST_CONTENT 、 RESPONSE 、 RESPONSE_CONTENT 。 对于REQUEST和RESPONSE范围，策略执行对内容没有查看或修改权限，这与REQUEST_CONTENT和RESPONSE_CONTENT不同。

我们不会深入探讨这个主题，但需要注意的重要一点是网关将首先执行标头策略（ REQUEST / RESPONSE ），然后执行内容策略（ REQUEST_CONTENT / RESPONSE_CONTENT ）。

让我们点击卡片来探索它提供的信息。

多亏了这种差异视图（我们将其称为“差异”），我们现在可以看到并理解分配属性是如何工作的：它在调用的内部上下文的 Attribute 对象中添加了一个属性。

那么现在，为什么我们的请求失败了？ 让我们滚动时间线并找到失败的策略。

这里是！ 我们可以看到 Json 到 Json 转换卡上出现了两个图标。 查看 Inspector，我们可以看到它发生在 Api > Body 上。

  • 表示策略有错误

查看差异，我们可以看到错误消息：“请求故意失败”。 这是我们在请求处理过程中看到和捕捉到的。 不幸的是，在这种情况下，它不是很有帮助。

然而，有趣的是Condition块：

我们可以看到条件无效。 再次阅读条件，看起来我们使用request.headeers而不是request.headers配置了我们的策略。

调试模式如何工作？

那么调试模式究竟是如何工作的呢？ 让我们来看看引擎盖下。

当您发送调试请求时，API Gateway 会检测到该事件并开始处理。
它将以我们称为“影子部署”的特定状态部署您当前的 API。 这只是意味着 API 已部署，但公众无法访问。 此部署仅由调试模式使用，这意味着您的客户（或任何其他用户！）访问此版本的 API 没有风险！

您无需以常规方式部署 API 即可对其进行调试。 当您进行一些测试以找到策略的良好调整时，这可能非常有用。

让我们看看它是如何工作的：

  1. 您将请求发送到调试模式请求表。
    • 然后 API 管理将创建一个调试事件并存储到数据库中
    • 事件的 ID 返回给用户
    • 然后，控制台 UI 将定期从 APIM REST API 轮询，直到事件状态为“SUCCESS”。
  2. 网关会定期与数据库同步，检查是否有状态为“TO_DEBUG”的事件
  3. 当发现一个事件时，我们以影子模式部署我们的 API。 这意味着两件事：
    • 任何用户都无法访问此 API，只能通过 Debug 模式访问，并且只能在 Debug 会话的生命周期内访问。
    • 该 API 采用特定封装构建，提供了在 API 的整个生命周期内存储执行信息的能力。
  4. 向此 API 发出带有用户调试请求内容的内部 HTTP 调用。 如果您尝试使用 cURL 或 Postman 调用您的 API，它具有精确复制会发生的情况的优势。
  5. 网关将照常处理您的请求并根据配置的策略转换数据。 与常规部署的唯一区别是有关标头、有效负载、上下文的信息将存储在调试上下文中
  6. 请求完成后，将取消部署 API，并将调试信息保存到事件中。
  7. 最后，结果会在控制台 UI 中显示给用户。

正如我们之前所说，条件拼写错误，因此我们现在已将 JSON To JSON 策略中的request.headers request.headeers

让我们在调试模式下再次调用：

响应为200 - OK ，我们可以在响应正文中看到应用的折扣。

调试模式还可以告诉我们策略是否由于未满足的条件而未执行。
如果我们不使用 api-key 或X-Library-Discount调用 API，则不会应用此 JSON 到 JSON 策略，如您在时间轴中所见：

包起来

我们刚刚完成了一个带有策略问题的失败 API 的简单示例，以及如何使用调试模式对其进行故障排除。 我们能够看到并逐步了解流程，以了解真正发生的事情以及导致问题的原因。 我们还研究了调试模式到底是如何工作的。

我们很想听听您的反馈，以及您如何使用调试模式。 如果您对该主题感兴趣并且有疑问，请不要犹豫加入我们的社区论坛。 团队将很乐意与您讨论！



原文:

If you're regularly working with APIs, you'll know the struggles of ensuring that they're secured, protected and managed, as well as building out the core functionality.

If you haven't already, you'll want to check out API Management. In essence,

API management is the process of creating and publishing web application programming interfaces (APIs), enforcing their usage policies, controlling access, nurturing the subscriber community, collecting and analyzing usage statistics, and reporting on performance. API Management components provide mechanisms and tools to support developer and subscriber communities

(see on Wikipedia).

Using the open source Gravitee.io API Management platform (GitHub repo) allows you to create, design and manage the lifecycle of your APIs. With many rich and powerful policies available, you have a huge array of tools and techniques to superpower your APIs, such as:

  • Check authentication and manage other security aspects
  • Transform headers
  • Protocol mediation
  • Protect your backends against malicious and accidental traffic spikes

But sometimes, your designs become so complex that it is almost impossible to know where design and configuration problems occur when they pop up. Don’t panic, Debug Mode is here to the rescue. Debug Mode is a feature that unleashes the power of API Publisher, allowing you to design an API, (shadow) deploy it (don’t worry, we’ll explain what shadow deployment is later on), and understand exactly through which policies have been applied to the API call.

In this blog post, we are going to put ourselves in the shoes of an API publisher, looking at an API displaying some weird behaviors. We will walk through a simple example of an API, and show you not only how to use Debug Mode, but also how it works under the hood.

Who am I ?

I am Yann Tavernier and I am a developer in the amazing APIM team. I had the opportunity to work on Debug Mode, and I’m delighted to walk you through and explain this feature!

Key concepts and components

Before we start looking at Debug Mode, let’s provide an overview of all of the key concepts and components that we’ll be using in this blog.

Key components:

  • APIM is the API Management solution made by Gravitee.io.
  • Gateway is the component of APIM responsible for handling requests from customers. As well acting as the proxy for APIs, it will also apply transformation, checks, rate limiting, and so forth on incoming requests and outgoing responses.

Key concepts:

  • API Publisher is a role type for APIM, representing the user of the company in charge of designing and publishing the APIs.
  • API is the representation of your backend inside APIM, and is composed of elements such as documentation, users, design, properties, etc.
  • Plan is the access and service layer on top of an API for consumer Applications. Consumer Applications need to subscribe to a plan to be able to consume an API.
  • Policy is a service or action that can be executed on an API request or response to perform checks, transformation or other services to it. As well as out of the box policies, you can also create your own.

Introducing our scenario

We are now going to introduce our simple scenario to walk through how to use Debug Mode.

All our examples will be done on a local installation. You can use this command to run APIM 3.17 with docker-compose:
cd /tmp && mkdir -p apim && cd apim && curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gravitee-io/gravitee-docker/master/apim/3.x/docker-compose.yml -o "docker-compose.yml" && export APIM_VERSION=3.17.1 && docker-compose down -v && docker-compose pull && docker-compose up

To access the console, go to the following address: http://localhost:8084

The default credentials are:

  • Login: admin
  • Password: admin

If you are interested in a more detailed guide, you can check this awesome blog from Ljubica Lazarevic!

We will rely on an API called Library backend. You can import it thanks to its API definition here.

This backend will be accessible on http://localhost:8082/library-backend and return mocked dataset:

{
   "books": [
       {
           "title": "A tale of two cities",
           "author": "Charles Dickens",
           "_internalReference": "gio_b1"
       },
       {
           "title": "The Lord of the Rings",
           "author": "J.R.R Tolkien",
           "_internalReference": "gio_b2"
       },
       {
           "title": "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy",
           "author": "Douglas Adams",
           "_internalReference": "gio_b3"
       }
   ]
}

We will analyze this dataset later.

As an API Publisher, I want to design a “Library” API to access my Library backend.
You can create it following the documentation and follow this guide step by step or you can directly import the resulting API Definition.
First of all, I need to choose the type of plan to use for my API. We will choose two plans: a Keyless plan, called Free, and an API-Key one, called Premium.

Let’s firstly design the Free plan. This plan allows anybody to reach our API. To protect our backend systems, as well as improve performance, we want to manage the amount of traffic that will hit our API with this plan. We will limit the access thanks to the Rate Limit policy. One thing to note - as this is a free plan, consuming applications do not need to create a subscription.

As the API is going to be publicly accessible, we will want to hide away some private data in the response, the _internalReference field. To do this, we’re going to use the JSON to JSON Transformation policy.

Here is the configuration of JsonToJson Policy. Its purpose is to remove the _internalReference field in the array of books:

[
  {
    "operation": "remove",
    "spec": {
      "books": {
        "*": {
          "_internalReference": ""
        }
      }
    }
  }
]

Let’s deploy the API (you can do this by synching the API when prompted) and call it with a cURL:
Calling curl http://localhost:8082/library will output:

{
   "books": [
       {
           "title": "A tale of two cities",
           "author": "Charles Dickens"
       },
       {
           "title": "The Lord of the Rings",
           "author": "J.R.R Tolkien"
       },
       {
           "title": "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy",
           "author": "Douglas Adams"
       }
   ]
}

We have successfully hidden away the sensitive, internal data that was being exposed by the API. However, our developer teams may well need access to that data for testing purposes. So what we will do now is create an Internal, API-Key plan, to allow authorized access to the data.It works by verifying the API key that is passed as a header or query parameter, and is linked to an application that has subscribed to the API. You can find out more in the documentation on how to set this up.

Remember, the _internalReference removal was done on the Freemium plan. Here, with the Internal plan, we keep the original data. We will use this opportunity to use the Assign Attributes policy to add an internal attribute in the context. This attribute will be the concatenation of the application’s ID followed by “-internal” and accessible through the “internal” key.

To do that, we configured the Assign Attribute policy to add an internal attribute with the following value: {#context.attributes["application"]}-internal.

Now, calling the API with an API Key with curl http://locahost:8082/library?api-key=7cc755d1-378b-4099-9650-9ee9330be55c will output:

{
   "books": [
       {
           "title": "A tale of two cities",
           "author": "Charles Dickens",
           "_internalReference": "gio_b1"
       },
       {
           "title": "The Lord of the Rings",
           "author": "J.R.R Tolkien",
           "_internalReference": "gio_b2"
       },
       {
           "title": "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy",
           "author": "Douglas Adams",
           "_internalReference": "gio_b3"
       }
   ]
}

NOTE: You may have noticed that we cannot see the attribute we set. In fact, the attribute only lives inside the API Gateway for the time of the call. This is a behavior of the policy we are using, and it is internal to the gateway.

Finally, we will create a flow on the API using the JSON to JSON transformation again to apply discounts to a book based on certain conditions.

The discount will be applied if the response is either:

  • Request is done with Header - “X-Library-Discount”:“discount percentage”
  • Attribute “internal” is not empty.

By default, the discount value will be the value of the X-Library-Discount header, or 10 percent by default.

To achieve that, we will firstly configure the condition of the policy:
{(#request.headeers['X-Library-Discount'] != null && #request.headers['X-Library-Discount'].size() > 0) || {#context.attributes['internal'] != null}}

Then, we will use this configuration for Json to Json Policy:

[
 {
   "operation": "shift",
   "spec": {
     "*": "&"
    }
 },
 {
   "operation": "default",
   "spec": {
     "discount": "{#request.headers['X-Library-Discount'] != null ? #request.headers['X-Library-Discount'][0] : 10}%"
    }
 }
]

Calling the API with curl http://localhost:8082/library?api-key=7cc755d1-378b-4099-9650-9ee9330be55c will output:

{
    "message":"Request failed unintentionally",
    "http_status_code":500
}

We have some issues with our last flow, but what happened? It’s hard to say by just using cURL or Postman. As the API design complexity increases, the risk of problems also increases. How can we avoid deploying our API to our users until we’ve solved all of the outstanding issues?

The APIM team created the new Debug Mode to solve this exact challenge.

Here comes the Debug Mode

The Debug Mode feature provides a way to have a global understanding and overview of what happens inside the gateway. With this feature, we are now able to send a request using our HTTP client, and see exactly which policies have been executed, with their inputs and outputs.

In the following screenshot, you can see an example of a request flow being examined using the Debug Mode:

The request is using the same API key as before, and we can see the Response ended in “500 - Internal Server Error”.

Let’s take a look at what the Debug Mode feature is showing us. The first important element of this feature is the timeline. It represents each policy that was run, shaped as a card. A card is composed of several elements:

  • The name of the policy (e.g. Assign Attributes)
  • Its stage (either: platform, security, plan, api) and scope (either: headers, body): Plan > Header
  • Its execution time (e.g. 0.227ms)

It represents the real order of execution of your policies, which may have changed from your initial design. You may notice that the execution could be different from what you expected!

If you’re a regular user of Gravitee, you are probably used to configuring policies and choosing one of the following scopes: REQUEST, REQUEST_CONTENT, RESPONSE, RESPONSE_CONTENT. For REQUEST and RESPONSE scopes, policy execution has no view or modification access on the content, contrary to REQUEST_CONTENT and RESPONSE_CONTENT.

We will not deep dive into this topic, but an important thing to note is that the gateway will first execute the header policies (REQUEST / RESPONSE) and then the content policies (REQUEST_CONTENT / RESPONSE_CONTENT).

Let’s click on the card to explore what information it provides.

Thanks to this differential view (we will call it a “diff”), we now can see and understand how the Assign Attributes works: it adds an attribute in the Attribute object of the internal context of the call.

So now, why did our request fail? Let’s scroll the timeline and find the failing policy.

Here it is! We can see two icons appearing on our Json to Json Transformation card. Looking at the Inspector, we can see it occurring on Api > Body.

  • IF… means the policy is conditional: it is executed only if the condition is evaluated to true.
  • ! means the policy has an error

Looking at the diff, we can see the error message: “Request failed intentionally”. This is what we saw and caught during the request processing. Unfortunately, in this case, it’s not very helpful.

The interesting thing, however, is the Condition block:

We can see that the condition is invalid. Reading the condition once more, it looks like we configured our policy using request.headeers instead of request.headers.

How does Debug Mode work ?

So how exactly does Debug Mode work? Let’s take a look under the hood.

When you send your Debug Request, the API Gateway detects the event, and it starts its processing.
It will deploy your current API in a particular state we call “Shadow Deployment”. This just means that the API is deployed, but not accessible to the public. This deployment is only used by Debug Mode, meaning there is no risk of your customers (or any other users!) accessing this version of your API!

You don’t need to deploy your API the regular way to be able to debug it. This can be really useful when you are doing some tests to find good tuning for a policy.

Let’s see it how it works:

  1. You send your request to the Debug Mode request form.
    • API Management will then create a Debug Event and store in to the database
    • The event’s ID is returned to the user
    • Then, the Console UI will poll regularly from APIM REST API until the event status is “SUCCESS”.
  2. The Gateway will regularly sync with the database to check if there are some events with the status “TO_DEBUG”
  3. When an event is found, we deploy our API in shadow mode. It implies two things:
    • This API is not accessible for any user, only by the Debug Mode and only for the lifetime of the Debug session.
    • This API is built with a particular encapsulation, providing the ability to store the execution information during all the lifecycle of the API.
  4. An internal HTTP call with the content of the user's debug request is made to this API. It has the advantage of exactly replicating what would happen if you tried to call your API with cURL or Postman.
  5. The gateway will process your request and transform data based on the configured policies, as usual. The only difference with a regular deployment is that information about headers, payload, context will be stored in the Debug context
  6. Once the request is finished, the API is undeployed, and debug information is saved to the event.
  7. Finally, the result is displayed to the user in the Console UI.

👨‍💻 Back to business

As we said earlier, the condition was misspelled, so we have now fixed request.headeers to request.headers in the JSON To JSON policy.

Let’s do the call once again in debug mode:

The response is 200 - OK, and we can see the discount applied in the response body.

Debug Mode can also tell us if a policy is not executing due to an unmet condition.
If we do not call the API with an api-key or X-Library-Discount, this JSON to JSON policy will not be applied, as you can see in the timeline:

Wrap up

We’ve just done a simple example of a failing API with policy issues, and how to troubleshoot it with Debug Mode. We were able to see and step through the flows to understand what was really happening and what was causing the problem. We have also looked at how exactly Debug Mode works under the hood.

We would love to hear your feedback, and how you’re getting on with Debug Mode. If the topic interests you and you have questions, do not hesitate to join our community forum. The team will be happy to discuss it with you!
item.author: ytvnr

item.title: 通过咖啡店示例了解异步任务☕ | Understand Asynchronous Tasks with Coffee Shop Example ☕
item.description:
发表于异步编程是一种并行编程形式，它允许工作单元与主应用程序线程分开运行。

当工作完成时，它会通知主线程。

让我们举一个现实生活中的例子来更好地理解这个概念。

咖啡店示例

  • 杰克：嗨。 请问我可以喝杯咖啡吗？ 第一位咖啡师：当然。 你想要别的东西吗？

  • 杰克：等咖啡准备好时吃一块蛋糕。 第一位咖啡师：当然。

第一服务员：还有什么事吗？

  • 杰克：没有。

  • 第一位咖啡师：请给我 5 美元。

  • 杰克：付钱坐下。

  • 第一位咖啡师：开始为下一位顾客服务。

  • 杰克：等着看推特吧。

  • 第二个咖啡师：这是你的蛋糕。 杰克：谢谢！

  • 第一位咖啡师：这是您的咖啡。 杰克：嘿，谢谢！ 拿走他的东西然后离开。

    • 简单来说，当你在等待别人为你做某事的时候，你可以做其他的事情，或者要求别人为你做更多的事情。

    这基本上是异步过程。

    使用bloggu.io发布的文章。 免费试用。



    原文:

    Mangabo Kolawole

    Posted on Apr 29

    Asynchronous programming is a form of parallel programming that allows a unit of work to run separately from the primary application thread.

    When the work is complete, it notifies the main thread.

    Let's take a real-life example to better understand the concept.

    Coffee Shop Example

    • Jack: Hi. Please can I have a coffee? (First asynchronous task)

    • First barista: For sure. Do you want something else?

    • Jack: A piece of cake while waiting for the coffee to be ready. (Second asynchronous task)

    • First barista: For sure.

    ( Launch the preparation of the coffee )

    • First Attendant: Anything else?

    • Jack: No.

    • First barista: 5 dollars, please.

    • Jack: Pay the money and take a seat.

    • First barista: Start serving the next customer.

    • Jack: Start checking Twitter while waiting.

    • Second barista: Here's your cake. (Second asynchronous task call returned.)

    • Jack: Thanks!

    • First barista: Here's your coffee. (First asynchronous task call returned.)

    • Jack: Hey, thanks! Take his stuff and leave.

    To make it simple, while you are waiting for someone else to do something for you, you can do other tasks or ask others to do more things for you.

    This is basically asynchronous process.

    Article posted using bloggu.io. Try it for free.
item.author: koladev

item.title: 为什么我们选择 Figma 作为我们的原型设计工具 | Why we chose Figma as our prototyping tool
item.description:
作为团队中唯一的设计师，我在第一季度所做的一件事就是尝试不同的原型制作工具，以找到适合我的设计工作流程的工具，并帮助我制作开发团队和业务团队可以用来评估的原型。产品。 该团队需要可点击的原型，可以处理来自 JSON 文件、API 或 google 表格文件的动态数据，以及可以处理输入、状态和逻辑的原型。 以下是我尝试过的原型制作工具以及我在使用它们时的经验。

成帧器

我对 Framer 的第一印象是，这是未来的原型制作工具。 我很高兴使用它，尤其是当我发现您可以编写自己的交互式组件并将它们添加到组件库时。 我记得当时想，设计团队和开发团队将使用同一个组件库，哇！ 但事实并非如此，因为您不能将大多数 npm 包导入 Framer，至少现在还不能，而且我们在Bit.dev中的组件库依赖于 antd。

另一种选择是以无代码方式重新创建 UI 组件，这似乎不是我的好时间，因为我已经在 Figma 中完成了带有悬停状态和自动布局的 UI 组件，以及我使用的组件'll recreate 将没有 Framer 与其内置组件的交互性。 不要误会我的意思，Framer 为可点击原型提供了出色的动画和过渡效果，但这不是优先事项。 我们想要具有状态的原型，可以处理动态数据，可以捕获输入数据并将其传递到另一个屏幕，并且可以使用 if-then 逻辑，而 Framer 不会为我们做这些。

我认为 Framer 是一个很棒的工具，有着光明的未来，但它不是 GetPaid 团队现在可以使用的工具，也许直到他们能够支持我们组件库中使用的 npm 包。开发。

与我之前学习和使用的其他工具相比，它有点不同。 我花了一段时间才弄到它，当我终于弄到它时，我意识到它不是一个好的设计工具，添加基本的设计细节需要花费太多精力，而且你甚至不能在那里进行混合模式。 因此，结合陡峭的学习等于解决 Figma 可点击原型。

结论

我认为这些工具不适合我的工作流程，因为在大多数情况下，我必须重做我已经在 Figma 中完成的设计工作，而且大多数时候我会得到不值得的结果。 借助 Figma 的原型制作工具，我能够实现出色的可点击原型，我们将作为整个团队使用，甚至在发布活动期间向我们的用户展示其中的一些原型。 设计文件仍然很乱，有很多原型线绕来绕去，我希望 if-then 调节是 Figma 可以在不久的将来实现的东西。



原文:

The end of March marked the end of Q1 here at GetPaid Africa. As the only designer in the team, one of the things that I did during Q1 was to try different prototyping tools to find the one that will fit my design workflow and help me produce prototypes that the development team and business team could use to evaluate the product. The team wanted clickable prototypes that can handle dynamic data from a JSON file, an API, or google sheets file, and one that can handle input, states, and logic. Here are the prototyping tools that I tried and my experience while using them.

Framer

My first impression of Framer was that this is the prototyping tool of the future. I was so excited to use it especially when I found out that you can code your own interactive components and add them to a component library. I remember thinking, the design team and the development team will be using the same component library, wow! But that wasn’t the case because you can’t import most npm packages to Framer, at least not yet and our component library in Bit.dev depends on antd.

Another option will be to recreate the UI components the no-code way, and that didn’t seem like a good usage of my time since I have UI components done in Figma already with hovers states and auto layout, and also the components that I’ll recreate won’t have that interactivity that Framer has with their built-in components. And don’t get me wrong, Framer has great animations and transitions for clickable prototypes, but that’s wasn’t the priority. We wanted prototypes that have states, can handle dynamic data, can capture input data and pass it to another screen, and can use if-then logic and Framer wasn’t going to do that for us.

I think Framer is a great tool, with a bright future ahead of it but it's not a tool that can be used by the GetPaid team for now, maybe until they are able to support npm packages that are used in our components library in bit.dev.

Axure RP

Axure RP seemed promising, it had most of the things that we needed including if-then conditioning which was huge. The challenge with Axure is that I found the learning curve to be very steep. It was a bit different compared to other tools that I have learned and used before. It took me a while to get it, and when I finally did, I realized that it is not a good design tool, takes too much effort to add basic design details, and you can’t even do blending modes in there. So that combined with steep learning equaled settling for Figma clickable prototypes.

Conclusion

I think that those tools didn’t fit in my work flow because in most instances I had to redo the design work that I had done in Figma already and I would most of the times get results that weren’t worth the hustle. With Figma’s prototyping tool, I was able to implement good clickable prototypes that we would use as the whole team and even show some of them to our users during our launch event. The design file is still messy, lots of prototype wires going around and I hope that if-then conditioning is something that Figma could implement in the near future.

item.title: React 设计模式：控制道具 | React Desing Pattern: Control Props
item.description:
何塞·惠洛克

发表于在 va avoir besoin de la notion de 'Controlled Component' 上注入设计模式。 吹捧简单，passons à l'implémentation：

第 1 步：启动 du 状态
const [name, setName] = useState("");

步骤 2: gestion du changement de valeur

 const handleName = (event) => { setName(event.target.value); };

第 3 步：控制 de l'élément

<input type="text" value={name} onChange={handleName} />

桑托什维斯瓦纳瑟姆 - Apr 16 4月16日



原文:

José Wheelock

Posted on Apr 29

Pour ce design pattern on va avoir besoin de la notion de 'Controlled Component'. Prenons comme exemple un formulaire avec un champ input, la valeur et le changement de ce champ est géré par le DOM.

Dans le cas d'un 'Controlled Component', c'est nous qui géront le stockage de la valeur et le onChange.

<input type="text" value={name} onChange={handleName} />

Tout simplement, passons à l'implémentation:

Step 1: Initiation du state
const [name, setName] = useState("");

Step 2: gestion du changement de valeur


const handleName = (event) => {
    setName(event.target.value);
  };

Step 3: controle de l'élément

<input type="text" value={name} onChange={handleName} />

Santosh Viswanatham - Apr 16
