item.title: 敏捷测试和 DevOps 如何相互关联？ | How do Agile Testing and DevOps interrelate?
item.description:
敏捷测试和 DevOps 经常被放在一起谈论，这是有充分理由的。 这两种方法都旨在促进开发和运营团队之间的协作和沟通，以便更快、更有效地交付软件。

那么敏捷测试和 DevOps 是如何相互关联的呢？

为了回答这个问题，我们必须首先了解每种方法的含义。

敏捷宣言强调个人和交互而不是流程和工具，因此软件测试人员和开发人员之间的协作是关键。 敏捷测试是一种确保在追求速度的过程中不牺牲质量的方法。 通过整合这两种方法，组织可以提高其整体效率和质量。 在软件开发方面，敏捷测试方法侧重于快速有效地交付高质量的软件。

为了做到这一点，测试人员需要能够与开发人员密切合作。 但是，这两种方法之间存在一些关键差异。 敏捷测试更关注测试过程本身，而 DevOps 更关注整个软件开发和交付过程。 话虽如此，敏捷测试和 DevOps 都有一些共同的目标，例如提高质量、缩短周期时间和提高客户满意度。 在组织内实施敏捷测试或 DevOps 时，重要的是要考虑该组织的独特需求和文化。

没有一种万能的解决方案，适用于一家公司的方法可能不适用于另一家公司。 最重要的是保持沟通畅通，不断尝试并从错误中吸取教训。 只有这样做，您才能为您的组织找到正确的前进道路。 敏捷测试的主要目标之一是确保软件没有缺陷并满足客户定义的要求。 这需要测试人员和开发人员之间的密切合作，因为缺陷通常在开发阶段本身就被识别和修复。

这两个概念是相辅相成的。 敏捷测试有助于确保软件满足客户要求，而 DevOps 使软件交付过程自动化，使其更快、更高效。 这两种方法都是软件开发团队在当今快节奏、竞争激烈的市场中取得成功所必需的。

结论 ：
当谈到敏捷测试与 DevOps 时，没有明显的赢家。 两种方法都有各自的优点和缺点，确定哪种方法适合您的组织的最佳方法是尝试并从错误中吸取教训。 最重要的是保持沟通畅通，并根据团队的反馈不断调整方法。 通过整合这两种方法，组织可以提高其整体效率和质量。 敏捷测试有助于确保不因追求速度而牺牲质量，而 DevOps 有助于改善测试人员和开发人员之间的协作。



原文:

Agile testing and DevOps are often spoken about in the same breath, and for good reason. Both are approaches that seek to promote collaboration and communication between development and operations teams in order to deliver software faster and more efficiently.

So how do Agile Testing and DevOps interrelate?

In order to answer that question, we must first understand what each methodology entails.

The Agile Manifesto emphasizes individuals and interactions over processes and tools, so collaboration between software testers and developers is key. DevOps is a methodology that aims to improve the collaboration between these two groups, so they can work more effectively together. agile testing is a way to ensure that quality is not sacrificed in the pursuit of speed. By integrating the two approaches, organizations can improve their overall efficiency and quality. When it comes to software development, the agile testing methodology focuses on delivering high-quality software quickly and efficiently.

In order to do this, testers need to be able to work closely with developers. DevOps is a methodology that helps to improve the collaboration between these two groups, so they can work more effectively together.

However, there are some key differences between the two approaches. Agile testing is more focused on the testing process itself, while DevOps is more concerned with the overall software development and delivery process. That being said, both Agile testing and DevOps share some common goals, such as improving quality, reducing cycle times, and increasing customer satisfaction. When it comes to implementing Agile testing or DevOps within an organization, it is important to consider the unique needs and culture of that organization.

There is no one-size-fits-all solution, and what works for one company may not work for another. The most important thing is to keep communication open and to constantly experiment and learn from your mistakes. Only by doing so will you be able to find the right path forward for your organization. One of the main goals of agile testing is to ensure that the software is defect-free and meets the requirements defined by the customer. This requires close collaboration between the testers and developers, as defects are often identified and fixed during the development phase itself.

DevOps is a methodology that aims to improve collaboration between developers and operations staff. The main goal of DevOps is to automate the software delivery process, so that new features can be delivered to customers faster and more reliably. The two concepts are complementary to each other. Agile testing helps to ensure that the software meets customer requirements, while DevOps automates the software delivery process, making it faster and more efficient. Both approaches are necessary for software development teams to be successful in today's fast-paced, competitive market.

Conclusion :
When it comes to agile testing vs DevOps, there is no clear winner. Both approaches have their own advantages and disadvantages, and the best way to determine which one is right for your organization is to experiment and learn from your mistakes. The most important thing is to keep communication open and to constantly adjust your approach based on feedback from your team. By integrating the two approaches, organizations can improve their overall efficiency and quality. agile testing helps to ensure that quality is not sacrificed in the pursuit of speed, while DevOps helps to improve the collaboration between testers and developers.
item.author: venessa_testrig

item.title: 重新思考我们的 Remix 管理路由 | Rethinking our Remix admin routes
item.description:
在上一篇文章中，我们首先介绍了 Remix 中的共享布局。

但是让我们更深入地看看我们如何可以稍微重构一下。

前面的示例使用/posts/admin结构，如果我们的管理部分中只有帖子，这很好。
但是如果我们添加类别会发生什么？ 现在我们必须在那里复制整个管理结构。

我想要实现的是一个单独的/admin/{something}结构。

注意：在大多数项目中，您会将管理​​员与前端部分完全分开。

在我们的例子中，我们将前端与管理部分混合起来，以简化我们的展示示例。

创建管理员路由

我们要更改的第一件事是拥有一个基本的管理文件夹。
在您的路线目录中创建此管理文件夹。

我们从上一篇文章中了解到，我们需要在路由目录中创建一个admin.tsx文件。 该文件将作为管理部分的主要布局。

继续创建此文件并使用以下标记。

 import { Link , Outlet } from ' @remix-run/react ' ; export default function PostAdmin () { return ( < div className = ' mx-auto max-w-4xl ' > < h1 className = ' my-6 mb-2 border-b-2 text-center text-3xl ' > My super cool admin < /h1 > < header className = ' border-b-2 mb-2 p-2 ' > < ul className = ' flex gap-3 ' > < li > < Link to = { ' /admin ' } className = ' text-blue-600 underline ' > Admin < /Link > < /li > < li > < Link to = { ' posts ' } className = ' text-blue-600 underline ' > Posts < /Link > < /li > < /ul > < /header > < main className = ' p-4 ' > < Outlet /> < /main > < /div > ); }

这将呈现一个基本标题，可以链接到我们的管理仪表板和我们的管理帖子部分。

如果我们现在在我们的admin文件夹中创建一个超级简单的index.tsx ，我们可以查看这个路由器插座是否工作。

 export default function AdminIndex () { return < p > Welcome to the admin panel < /p> ; }

继续运行您的应用程序。 此时，您应该会看到以下结果。

该图像呈现了大标题、带有两个链接的菜单以及插座内的索引文件。

创建管理员帖子部分

现在我们可以在我们的管理文件夹中创建一个posts文件夹。 这将是我们现有的帖子管理员的家。

同样，我们可以利用特定的布局方法在我们的 admin 文件夹中创建一个posts.tsx文件。 这将再次成为我们的布局。

如果你跟着，你已经有了这个布局，因为它以前是posts/admin.tsx文件。

移动并重命名此文件，或复制粘贴以下代码。

 import { json } from " @remix-run/node " ; import { Link , Outlet , useLoaderData } from " @remix-run/react " ; import { getPosts } from " ~/models/post.server " ; import { LoaderFunction } from " @remix-run/node " ; type LoaderData = { posts : Awaited < ReturnType < typeof getPosts >> ; }; export const loader : LoaderFunction = async () => { return json ({ posts : await getPosts () }); }; export default function PostAdmin () { const { posts } = useLoaderData () as LoaderData ; return ( < div className = " grid grid-cols-4 gap-6 " > < nav className = " col-span-4 md:col-span-1 " > < ul > { posts . map (( post ) => ( < li key = { post . slug } > < Link to = { `/posts/ ${ post . slug } ` } className = " text-blue-600 underline " > { post . title } < /Link > < /li > ))} < /ul > < /nav > < main className = " col-span-4 md:col-span-3 " > < Outlet /> < /main > < /div > ); }

在admin/posts文件夹中，创建index.tsx文件，这是我们发布部分的欢迎屏幕。

 import { Link } from ' @remix-run/react ' ; export default function AdminIndex () { return ( < p > < Link to = ' new ' className = ' text-blue-600 underline ' > Create a New Post < /Link > < /p > ); }

然后我们还想使用我们也创建的新帖子页面，您可以简单地将new.tsx文件移动到这个admin/posts文件夹或使用以下内容创建一个新的：

 import { Form } from ' @remix-run/react ' ; import { redirect } from ' @remix-run/node ' ; import { createPost } from ' ~/models/post.server ' ; const inputClassName = `w-full rounded border border-gray-500 px-2 py-1 text-lg` ; export const action = async ({ request }) => { const formData = await request . formData (); const title = formData . get ( ' title ' ); const slug = formData . get ( ' slug ' ); const content = formData . get ( ' content ' ); await createPost ({ title , slug , content }); return redirect ( ' /posts/admin ' ); }; export default function NewPost () { return ( < Form method = ' post ' > < p > < label > Post Title :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' title ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < p > < label > Post Slug :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' slug ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < p > < label > Content :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' content ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < p className = ' text-right ' > < button type = ' submit ' className = ' rounded bg-blue-500 py-2 px-4 text-white hover:bg-blue-600 focus:bg-blue-400 disabled:bg-blue-300 ' > Create Post < /button > < /p > < /Form > ); }

就是这样。 我们现在将我们的帖子管理部分迁移到了全局管理部分。

这使得在稍后阶段添加更多管理选项变得更加容易。

你可以在GitHub 上找到完整的代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

In the previous article, we first looked at shared layouts in Remix.

But let's take a deeper look at how we can refactor this a little bit.

The previous example used a /posts/admin structure, which is fine if we only have posts in our admin section.
But what happens if we add categories? Now we have to replicate the whole admin structure there.

What I want to achieve is a separate /admin/{something} structure.

Note: You would fully split your admin from the front-end part in most projects.

In our case, we will mix the front-end with the admin part to ease our showcase example.

Creating the admin routes

The first thing we want to change is to have a basic admin folder.
Create this admin folder inside your routes directory.

We have learned from the previous article that we will need to create an admin.tsx file in our routes directory. This file will serve as the main layout for the admin part.

Go ahead and create this file and use the following markup.

import { Link, Outlet } from '@remix-run/react'; export default function PostAdmin() { return ( <div className='mx-auto max-w-4xl'> <h1 className='my-6 mb-2 border-b-2 text-center text-3xl'> My super cool admin </h1>
 <header className='border-b-2 mb-2 p-2'> <ul className='flex gap-3'> <li> <Link to={'/admin'} className='text-blue-600 underline'> Admin </Link>
 </li>
 <li> <Link to={'posts'} className='text-blue-600 underline'> Posts </Link>
 </li>
 </ul>
 </header>
 <main className='p-4'> <Outlet /> </main>
 </div>
 );
}

This will render a basic header that can link to our admin dashboard and our admin post section.

If we now create a super simple index.tsx inside our admin folder, we can see if this router outlet works.

export default function AdminIndex() { return <p>Welcome to the admin panel</p>;
}

Go ahead and run your app. At this point, you should see the following result.

The image rendered the big header, the menu with the two links, and the index file inside the outlet.

Creating the admin post section

Now we can create a posts folder inside our admin folder. This will be the home for our existing post admin.

And again, we can leverage the specific layout methods to create a posts.tsx file in our admin folder. (So one level up from the admin/posts/ folder)

This will be our layout again.

If you are following along, you already have this layout as it was previously the posts/admin.tsx file.

Move and rename this file, or copy-paste the below code.

import { json } from "@remix-run/node";
import { Link, Outlet, useLoaderData } from "@remix-run/react";
import { getPosts } from "~/models/post.server";
import { LoaderFunction } from "@remix-run/node"; type LoaderData = { posts: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getPosts>>;
}; export const loader: LoaderFunction = async () => { return json({ posts: await getPosts() });
}; export default function PostAdmin() { const { posts } = useLoaderData() as LoaderData; return ( <div className="grid grid-cols-4 gap-6"> <nav className="col-span-4 md:col-span-1"> <ul> {posts.map((post) => ( <li key={post.slug}> <Link to={`/posts/${post.slug}`} className="text-blue-600 underline" > {post.title} </Link>
 </li>
 ))} </ul>
 </nav>
 <main className="col-span-4 md:col-span-3"> <Outlet /> </main>
 </div>
 );
}

Inside the admin/posts folder, create the index.tsx file, our welcome screen for the post section.

import { Link } from '@remix-run/react'; export default function AdminIndex() { return ( <p> <Link to='new' className='text-blue-600 underline'> Create a New Post </Link>
 </p>
 );
}

And then we also want to use the new post page, which we also created, you can simply move the new.tsx file to this admin/posts folder or create a new one with this content:

import { Form } from '@remix-run/react';
import { redirect } from '@remix-run/node';
import { createPost } from '~/models/post.server'; const inputClassName = `w-full rounded border border-gray-500 px-2 py-1 text-lg`; export const action = async ({ request }) => { const formData = await request.formData(); const title = formData.get('title'); const slug = formData.get('slug'); const content = formData.get('content'); await createPost({ title, slug, content }); return redirect('/posts/admin');
}; export default function NewPost() { return ( <Form method='post'> <p> <label> Post Title:{' '} <input type='text' name='title' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 <p> <label> Post Slug:{' '} <input type='text' name='slug' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 <p> <label> Content:{' '} <input type='text' name='content' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 <p className='text-right'> <button type='submit' className='rounded bg-blue-500 py-2 px-4 text-white hover:bg-blue-600 focus:bg-blue-400 disabled:bg-blue-300' > Create Post </button>
 </p>
 </Form>
 );
}

And that's it. We now migrated our post admin section to a global admin section.

This makes it easier to add some more admin options at a later stage.

You can find the completed code on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter
item.author: dailydevtips1

item.title: 提交时自动进行代码审查 | Automating code review on commit
item.description:
想象一下每天花费 1-4 小时进行代码审查的忙碌。 代码审查自动化可以节省无数浪费的精力。

本文可能会引导您逐步了解如何添加可在每次代码提交期间执行的自动代码审查。

使用 Codacy，您可以在每次提交和拉取请求中获得静态分析、圈复杂度、重复和代码单元测试覆盖率变化。

将您的项目与 Codacy 集成的步骤

您可以使用您的 Git 提供商（例如 GitHub、GitLab 或 Bitbucket）来注册 Codacy。 这会将您的 Codacy 用户与您的 Git 提供者用户链接起来，从而更容易将存储库添加到 Codacy 并邀请您的团队成员。

现在您将需要加入包含您的存储库的组织

笔记：

示例 如果您的存储库是“github.com/Fruitsandvegies”。 然后您的组织名称将是“Fruitsandvegies”。

通过在步骤 3 中选择组织，codency 将列出所有可用的相关存储库。 选择“添加”选项以添加存储库以供审核。 笔记：

仅当您有权访问该存储库时，才能将存储库添加到 Codancy。

分析完成查看结果后，选择“转到存储库”。

详细分析结果

The initial analysis from Codancy will be based on the default configuration. Codancy 的初始分析将基于默认配置。 Codacy provides tweaks to allow you to set up a custom configuration such as files to ignore, code pattern, and adjust quality settings. Codacy 提供调整以允许您设置自定义配置，例如要忽略的文件、代码模式和调整质量设置。



原文:

Imagine the hustle of spending 1- 4 hours on code review every day. Code review automation may save countless wasted efforts.

This Article may walk you through steps on how to add automated code reviews that may be executed during every code commit.

Codacy is one of the tools that may be used to achieve this.

Codacy is an automated code review tool that helps developers ship better software, faster. With Codacy, you get static analysis, cyclomatic complexity, duplication, and code unit test coverage changes in every commit and pull request.

Steps to integrate your project with Codacy

1. Sign up

You may use your Git provider such as GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket to sign up to Codacy. This links your Codacy user with your Git provider user, making it easier to add repositories to Codacy and invite your teammates.

2. Grant access

Codacy will request access to your Git provider and will require you to provide a few details regarding your organization. (this helps them on use case evaluation).

3. Choose organization

Now you will be required to join the organizations that contain your repositories

Note:

codacy name the organizations similar to your git repository user name.

Example If your repository is “github.com/Fruitsandvegies”. Then the name of your organization will be “Fruitsandvegies”.

4. Adding repository

By selecting the organization on step 3, codency will list all related repositories available. Select the “add” option to add the repository for review. Codancy will immediately set up everything so as to be ready to analyze on the next commit.

Note:

You can add the repository to Codancy only if you have permission to that repository.

5. Review result of the analysis

Select “Go to repository” once the analysis has completed reviewing the results.

Detailed analysis result

The initial analysis from Codancy will be based on the default configuration. Codacy provides tweaks to allow you to set up a custom configuration such as files to ignore, code pattern, and adjust quality settings.
item.author: neicore

item.title: 为什么我们选择 Figma 作为我们的原型设计工具 | Why we chose Figma as our prototyping tool
item.description:
作为团队中唯一的设计师，我在第一季度所做的一件事就是尝试不同的原型制作工具，以找到适合我的设计工作流程的工具，并帮助我制作开发团队和业务团队可以用来评估的原型。产品。 该团队需要可点击的原型，可以处理来自 JSON 文件、API 或 google 表格文件的动态数据，以及可以处理输入、状态和逻辑的原型。 以下是我尝试过的原型制作工具以及我在使用它们时的经验。

成帧器

我对 Framer 的第一印象是，这是未来的原型制作工具。 我很高兴使用它，尤其是当我发现您可以编写自己的交互式组件并将它们添加到组件库时。 我记得当时想，设计团队和开发团队将使用同一个组件库，哇！ 但事实并非如此，因为您不能将大多数 npm 包导入 Framer，至少现在还不能，而且我们在Bit.dev中的组件库依赖于 antd。

另一种选择是以无代码方式重新创建 UI 组件，这似乎不是我的好时间，因为我已经在 Figma 中完成了带有悬停状态和自动布局的 UI 组件，以及我使用的组件'll recreate 将没有 Framer 与其内置组件的交互性。 不要误会我的意思，Framer 为可点击原型提供了出色的动画和过渡效果，但这不是优先事项。 我们想要具有状态的原型，可以处理动态数据，可以捕获输入数据并将其传递到另一个屏幕，并且可以使用 if-then 逻辑，而 Framer 不会为我们做这些。

我认为 Framer 是一个很棒的工具，有着光明的未来，但它不是 GetPaid 团队现在可以使用的工具，也许直到他们能够支持我们组件库中使用的 npm 包。开发。

与我之前学习和使用的其他工具相比，它有点不同。 我花了一段时间才弄到它，当我终于弄到它时，我意识到它不是一个好的设计工具，添加基本的设计细节需要花费太多精力，而且你甚至不能在那里进行混合模式。 因此，结合陡峭的学习等于解决 Figma 可点击原型。

结论

我认为这些工具不适合我的工作流程，因为在大多数情况下，我必须重做我已经在 Figma 中完成的设计工作，而且大多数时候我会得到不值得的结果。 借助 Figma 的原型制作工具，我能够实现出色的可点击原型，我们将作为整个团队使用，甚至在发布活动期间向我们的用户展示其中的一些原型。 设计文件仍然很乱，有很多原型线绕来绕去，我希望 if-then 调节是 Figma 可以在不久的将来实现的东西。



原文:

The end of March marked the end of Q1 here at GetPaid Africa. As the only designer in the team, one of the things that I did during Q1 was to try different prototyping tools to find the one that will fit my design workflow and help me produce prototypes that the development team and business team could use to evaluate the product. The team wanted clickable prototypes that can handle dynamic data from a JSON file, an API, or google sheets file, and one that can handle input, states, and logic. Here are the prototyping tools that I tried and my experience while using them.

Framer

My first impression of Framer was that this is the prototyping tool of the future. I was so excited to use it especially when I found out that you can code your own interactive components and add them to a component library. I remember thinking, the design team and the development team will be using the same component library, wow! But that wasn’t the case because you can’t import most npm packages to Framer, at least not yet and our component library in Bit.dev depends on antd.

Another option will be to recreate the UI components the no-code way, and that didn’t seem like a good usage of my time since I have UI components done in Figma already with hovers states and auto layout, and also the components that I’ll recreate won’t have that interactivity that Framer has with their built-in components. And don’t get me wrong, Framer has great animations and transitions for clickable prototypes, but that’s wasn’t the priority. We wanted prototypes that have states, can handle dynamic data, can capture input data and pass it to another screen, and can use if-then logic and Framer wasn’t going to do that for us.

I think Framer is a great tool, with a bright future ahead of it but it's not a tool that can be used by the GetPaid team for now, maybe until they are able to support npm packages that are used in our components library in bit.dev.

Axure RP

Axure RP seemed promising, it had most of the things that we needed including if-then conditioning which was huge. The challenge with Axure is that I found the learning curve to be very steep. It was a bit different compared to other tools that I have learned and used before. It took me a while to get it, and when I finally did, I realized that it is not a good design tool, takes too much effort to add basic design details, and you can’t even do blending modes in there. So that combined with steep learning equaled settling for Figma clickable prototypes.

Conclusion

I think that those tools didn’t fit in my work flow because in most instances I had to redo the design work that I had done in Figma already and I would most of the times get results that weren’t worth the hustle. With Figma’s prototyping tool, I was able to implement good clickable prototypes that we would use as the whole team and even show some of them to our users during our launch event. The design file is still messy, lots of prototype wires going around and I hope that if-then conditioning is something that Figma could implement in the near future.
item.author: bmsteven

item.title: 了解 Kubernetes：第 46 部分 – 在容器中执行命令 | Understanding Kubernetes: part 46 – Execute commands in containers
item.description:
奥雷利·瓦什

发表于了解 Kubernetes 可能很困难或很耗时。 为了传播有关云技术的知识，我开始创建有关 Kubernetes 的草图。 我认为这可能是解释 Kubernetes（和其他技术）的好方法，更直观。

我们继续关于 Kubernetes 的 Sketchnotes 系列，重点介绍如何在容器中的 Pod 内执行命令。

如果你有兴趣，我发布了关于 Kubernetes 的所有草图（以及新的！），制作了一本 173 页的“书”： “以视觉方式理解 Kubernetes” 。

像往常一样，如果你喜欢这些速写笔记，可以关注我，告诉我你的想法。 我将很快发布其他草图:-)。

高级食欲信息 - Mar 31 3月31日



原文:

Aurélie Vache

Posted on Apr 29

Understanding Kubernetes can be difficult or time-consuming. In order to spread knowledges about Cloud technologies I started to create sketchnotes about Kubernetes. I think it could be a good way, more visual, to explain Kubernetes (and others technologies).

We continue the serie of Sketchnotes about Kubernetes, with a focus on how to execute commands inside a Pod in a container.

If you are interested, I published all the sketchnotes on Kubernetes (and new ones!) to make a "book" of 173 pages: "Understanding Kubernetes in a visual way".

As usual, if you like theses sketchnotes, you can follow me, and tell me what do you think. I will publish others sketchs shortly :-).

advancedappetiteinfo - Mar 31
item.author: aurelievache

item.title: 质量保证分析师-您的职业道路！ | Quality Assurance Analyst- Career Path For You!
item.description:
塔兰维尔·辛格

发表于随着 IT 工作数量的增加，越来越多的人正在考虑成为 QA 分析师。 但是，在您冒险踏上这样的专业道路之前，您应该更多地了解质量保证分析师的工作。

一个称职的 QA 分析师对技术的工作原理感到好奇，喜欢深入了解细节，并且是更广泛的事物方案中的自然问题解决者。 这项工作需要花费大量时间准备测试计算机软件并解决充分发展的问题。

本文将教您有关软件测试的所有知识。 对于有兴趣从事软件测试职业的人来说，这是一本必读的书。

什么是质量保证？

质量保证是一个测试系统，可确保客户收到没有严重缺陷的高质量软件。 与开发团队一样，他们的使命是提供最好的产品。 每个软件组织，从亚马逊和谷歌到小型初创企业，都需要质量保证。

什么是质量分析师的工作描述？

质量分析师负责协助计划、设计和执行从简单到复杂的项目的系统测试。 质量分析师与 IT 部门和其他业务部门合作，根据系统要求创建和验证测试用例。

为什么公司需要质量分析师？

在当今世界，许多企业都在聘用质量分析师。 质量分析师负责所有先前的工作，从计划到执行，然后检查产​​品以确保按时交付。 公司必须通过按时完成任务来满足其客户和消费者。 因此，质量分析师可以轻松处理所有职责和工作任务。

它帮助他们塑造公司的未来发展，检查测试过程，并在为时已晚之前解决任何与产品相关的问题。 因此，聘请质量分析师对于想要产生高质量结果的公司来说是一项明智的投资。

哪个是学习 QA 测试的最佳平台？

注册 JanBask Training 的手动测试自动化，以了解 QA 软件测试生命周期并获得各种级别的测试的实践经验，从使用 Selenium 和 Maven 的手动测试到自动测试。 为自己配备通过 QA 软件测试证书所需的知识和能力，您将离雄心勃勃的工作更近一步。

谁应该参加 Janbask QA 培训？

如果你是——你应该参加 JanBask 课程——

你才刚刚开始，不知道从哪里开始。 你在一个稳定的位置，但你需要扩大我的视野。 如果您想提高自己的技能并掌握课程，那么这里就是您的理想去处。

您是否渴望改善现有或新工作？

我们的 QA 测试课程的主要目标是：

提供在线 QA 认证课程，提供与传统课堂相似的体验，并减轻学生/学习者参加体育课的压力，以及他们的金钱、精力和时间。 通过解决 QA 测试员认证和工作角色所需的概念、技能和实践，为他们提供对 QA 测试学科的透彻理解。

通过身临其境的、令人兴奋的电子学习工具和行业专家主导的课程，提供统一和全面的培训，传授 QA 测试技术和流程的基本到高级概念/技能（在QA 测试在线认证考试中针对个人进行检查） .


向您展示如何通过定期监控和测试每个业务应用程序、流程和系统来避免错误、默认值和性能问题。

典型的责任和义务

  • 在许多不同规模的项目上，制定测试策略、测试用例、测试脚本和测试报告。
  • 对各种软件、电信和报告系统进行测试。
  • 识别并协助职能和技术小组解决问题。
  • 管理软件 beta 测试计划。
  • 确保在测试过程中满足用户假设。

资格和技能

即使是入门级工作也需要计算机科学或密切相关领域的学士学位。 对于中级和高级职位，可能需要了解软件和软件开发流程、测试协议、错误报告工具以及手动和自动测试。 沟通能力、解决问题的能力和独立工作的能力在任何层面都是有价值的属性。

一些技术技能是-

  • 需要熟悉数据库/SQL。
  • 需要基本的 Linux 命令专业知识。
  • 测试管理工具知识和实践经验
  • 需要任何缺陷跟踪工具知识和实践经验。
  • 自动化工具知识和实践专业知识

典型的软件测试雇主和平均工资

软件开发公司尤其需要软件测试和质量保证专家。 在印度，年薪从 22.5 万卢比到 90 万卢比（48,000 美元到 105,000 美元）不等。 工作环境明亮企业化，工作自由、学习机会和领导前景。 然而，由于持续的测试操作，工作本身可能会随着时间的推移变得乏味。

选择职业

在当前情况下，质量分析师处于独特的位置。 随着越来越多的公司采用更多的合作态度和项目方法，质量分析师越来越多地参与项目开发的每一步。

由于这种独特的视角，质量分析师的工作可能会导致其他 IT 职业，如产品管理、企业架构或客户体验领导。 遵循整体 DevOps 方法进行项目开发的公司也可以转变为发布经理或工程师等角色。

培养优秀质量分析师的素质可以导致 IT 管理中的角色，例如 IT 经理、首席技术官和首席信息官。 质量分析师和 IT 经理都必须着眼大局，批判性地思考发现和解决问题。

替代软件测试职业道路

在您涉足手动测试之后，您可以探索以下专业。

自动化测试：作为自动化测试工程师，您将负责自动执行手动测试用例，否则这些用例会很耗时。 性能测试：作为性能测试工程师，您将负责确保应用程序具有响应性（加载时间、程序可以处理的最大负载等）。 业务分析师：测试人员相对于开发人员的主要优势之一是他们对业务的全面了解。 作为业务分析师的职业是测试人员选择的明显途径。 作为业务分析师，您将负责研究和分析贵公司的业务模型和工作流程。 作为 BA，您将使用技术来集成这些模型和工作流程。

结论
公司渴望尽快将他们的软件应用程序推向市场，这需要雇用熟练的测试人员和质量保证人员。 此外，测试人员和 QA 工程师经常比开发人员更了解广泛的业务原则，许多公司都重视这种知识。 您应该了解qa 认证。



原文:

Taranveer Singh

Posted on Apr 29

More people are considering becoming QA analysts as the number of IT jobs increases. However, before you take the plunge and embark on such a professional path, you should learn more about what a quality assurance analyst performs.

A competent QA analyst is curious about how technology works, appreciates getting into the nitty-gritty and is a natural problem solver in the broader scheme of things. The job necessitates a significant amount of time spent preparing to test computer software and resolve issues that develop adequately.

This article will teach you all you need to know about software testing. This is a must-read for anyone interested in pursuing a career in software testing.

What is QA?

Quality assurance is a testing system that ensures that customers receive high-quality software free of critical flaws. QA testers dream of producing bug-free software, yet flaws are unavoidable in the software development process. Their mission, like that of the development team, is to deliver the best possible product. Every software organization, from Amazon and Google to small start-ups, needs quality assurance.

What is a Quality Analyst's job description?

The quality analyst is in charge of assisting in the planning, design, and execution of system testing on projects ranging from simple to complicated. The quality analyst collaborates with the IT department and other business units to create and validate test cases based on system requirements.

Why do companies require a Quality Analyst?

In today's world, many businesses are employing quality analysts. A quality analyst is in charge of all prior work, from planning through execution and then inspecting the product to ensure it is delivered on time. A company must satisfy its clients and consumers by completing its task on time. As a result, a quality analyst can handle all of the duties and work tasks with ease.

It assists them in shaping the company's future development, checking the testing process, and resolving any product-related issues before they become too late. As a result, hiring a quality analyst is a sensible investment for a firm that wants to produce high-quality results.

Which is the Best Platform for Learning QA Testing?

Sign up for JanBask Training's manual testing automation to learn about the QA Software Testing life-cycle and get hands-on experience with various levels of testing, from manual to automated testing using Selenium and Maven. Equip yourself with the knowledge and abilities you'll need to pass the QA Software Testing Certificates, and you'll be one step closer to your ambitious job.

Who should attend Janbask QA Training?

You should attend JanBask classes if you are-

You're just getting started and don't know where to begin. You are in a stable position, yet you need to expand my horizons. If you're looking to brush up on your skills and master the course, this is the place to be.

Are you eager to improve your existing or new job?

Objectives of JanBask QA Testing Certification

Our QA testing course's primary goal is to:

Provide an online QA certification course that provides experiences similar to those found in traditional classrooms and relieves students/learners of the stress of going to physical classes, as well as their dollars, energy, and time. To provide them with a thorough understanding of the QA Testing discipline by addressing the concepts, skills, and practices required by a QA tester certification and job role.

To provide a unified and comprehensive training that imparts basic to advanced concepts/skills of QA Testing techniques and processes – which are examined for an individual in the QA Testing online Certification examination – through immersive, exciting e-learning tools and industry experts-led classes.


To show you how to avoid bugs, defaults, and performance problems by regularly monitoring and testing each business application, process, and system.

Typical Responsibilities and Duties

  • On many projects of various sizes, develop test strategies, test cases, test scripts, and test reports.
  • Carry out testing on a variety of software, telecommunications, and reporting systems.
  • Identify and assist functional and technical groups in resolving issues.
  • Manage software beta-testing initiatives.
  • Ensure that user assumptions are met during the testing process.

Qualifications and Skills

Even entry-level jobs demand a bachelor's degree in computer science or a closely related field. For middle-level and senior positions, knowledge of software and software development processes, testing protocols, bug-reporting tools, and manual and automated testing may be necessary. Communication skills, problem-solving talents, and the ability to work independently are all valuable attributes at any level.

Some Technical Skills are-

  • Database/SQL familiarity is required.
  • Basic Linux command expertise is required.
  • Test Management Tool knowledge and hands-on experience
  • Any Defect Tracking tool knowledge and hands-on experience are required.
  • Automation tool knowledge and hands-on expertise

Typical software testing employers and average salaries

Software testing and quality assurance experts are particularly in demand in software development firms. In India, annual salaries range from Rs 2.25 lakh to Rs 9 lakh ($48,000 to $105,000). The work environment is brightly corporate, with job freedom, learning possibilities, and leadership prospects. However, because of the continual testing operations, the job itself may grow tedious with time.

Choosing a Career

Quality Analysts are in a distinctive position in the present scenario. Quality Analysts are becoming more involved in every step of a project's development as more firms adopt more cooperative attitudes and project methodologies.

Because of this unique perspective, a job as a Quality Analyst might lead to other IT occupations such as product management, enterprise architecture, or customer experience leadership. Firms that follow a holistic DevOps approach to project development can also transition into roles such as release managers or engineers.

The qualities that create an excellent Quality Analyst can lead to roles in IT management, such as IT managers, Chief Technology Officers, and Chief Information Officers. Both quality analysts and IT managers must look at the big picture and think critically about detecting and resolving issues.

Alternative Software Testing Career Paths

After you've dabbled in manual testing, you can explore the following specialties.

Automation Testing: As an Automation Test Engineer, you'll be in charge of automating the execution of manual test cases that would otherwise be time-consuming. IBM Rational Robot, Silk Performer, and QTP were used as tools.

Performance Testing: As a performance test engineer, you'll be in charge of ensuring that applications are responsive (load time, the maximum load the program can handle, and so on). WEBLoad and Loadrunner were used as tools.

Business Analyst: One of the primary advantages that Testers have over Developers is their complete understanding of the business. A career as a Business Analyst is an obvious path for testers to choose. You will be responsible for studying and analyzing your company's business model and workflows as a Business Analyst. As a BA, you will use technology to integrate these models and workflows.

Conclusion
Companies are eager to get their software applications out into the market as fast as possible, which necessitates hiring skilled testers and quality assurance personnel. Furthermore, testers and QA engineers are frequently found to understand broad business principles better than developers, and many firms value this knowledge. You should learn about qa certifications.

item.title: Mojolicious 的喜欢/不喜欢系统 | Like/Dislike system with Mojolicious
item.description:
阿斯隆加

发表于这篇文章描述了基于 Mojolicious 的程序中实际的 Like/Dislike 机制。 网页样式是使用 Bootstrap 框架创建的。 因此，在启动包Vote.pm中，我们在名为'first-section' （例如）中为具有特定 ID 的帖子创建路由器，并为like和dislike创建路由器：

 sub startup { my $self = shift; my $r = $self->routes; ... $r->any('/first-section/:id' => [ id => qr/[0-9]+/ ] )->to('post#article'); ... $r->any('/likeartcl')->to('voting#like'); $r->any('/dislikeartcl')->to('voting#dislike'); ... }

包Session.pm ：

 package Session; use Mojo::Base -base; #--------------------------------- sub user { #--------------------------------- my($self, $c, $login, $password, $id) = @_; $c->session( client => [$login, $password, $id], expiration => 120); return 1; }#--------------- # 'voting' method of Session called in 'Voting.pm' package (see code fragment below) #--------------------------------- sub voting { #--------------------------------- my($self, $c, $user_vote_id, $title_alias_and_id) = @_; $c->signed_cookie( $user_vote_id => $title_alias_and_id, {expires => time + 120}); return 1; }#--------------- #--------------------------------- sub client_expire { #--------------------------------- my($self, $c) = @_; delete $c->session->{'client'}; return 1; }#--------------- 1;

打包投票/Voting.pm
在这里，我们渲染模板'voting/vote.html.ep' ，它描述了带有'like'和'dislike'按钮以及'like'和'dislike'计数的代码块

package Vote::Voting; use Mojo::Base 'Mojolicious::Controller'; use Mojo::Util qw(trim encode decode); use Mojo::Cookie; use Session; #--------------------------------- sub like { #--------------------------------- my $self = shift; my($nickname, $status); eval{ $nickname = $self->session('client')->[0]; }; my $client_check = SessCheck->client( $self, $nickname ); my $title_alias = $self->param('title_alias'); my $article_id = $self->param('article_id'); my $user_id = $self->param('user_id'); $status = !$client_check ? ' disabled' : ''; my $like_count = $self->db->select( 'posts', ['liked'], {id => $article_id} ) ->hash->{liked}; my $unlike_count = $self->db->select( 'posts', ['unliked'], {id => $article_id} ) ->hash->{unliked}; my $like_cookie_name = 'like_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id; my $unlike_cookie_name = 'unlike_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id; if( !$self->signed_cookie( $like_cookie_name ) && $client_check ){ ++$like_count; $self->db->update( 'posts', {'liked' => $like_count}, {'id' => $article_id} ); } if( $self->signed_cookie( $unlike_cookie_name ) ){ --$unlike_count; $self->db->update( 'posts', {'unliked' => $unlike_count}, {'id' => $article_id} ); $self->signed_cookie( $unlike_cookie_name => '', {expires => 1}); } # Store session for 'like' action where key is #'like_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id # and value is $title_alias.'_'.$article_id Session->voting( $self, 'like_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, $title_alias.'_'.$article_id ); $self->render( template => 'voting/vote', section_ident => $title_alias, id => $article_id, like_id => 'like_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, unlike_id => 'unlike_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, client_id => $user_id, unlike_btn_name => 'unlike'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, like_btn_name => 'like'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, status => $status, liked_cnt => $like_count, unliked_cnt => $unlike_count ); }#--------------- #--------------------------------- sub dislike { #--------------------------------- my $self = shift; my($nickname, $status); eval{ $nickname = $self->session('client')->[0]; }; my $client_check = SessCheck->client( $self, $nickname ); my $title_alias = $self->param('title_alias'); my $article_id = $self->param('article_id'); my $user_id = $self->param('user_id'); $status = !$client_check ? ' disabled' : ''; my $unlike_count = $self->db->select( 'posts', ['unliked'], {id => $article_id} ) ->hash->{unliked}; my $like_count = $self->db->select( 'posts', ['liked'], {id => $article_id} ) ->hash->{liked}; my $like_cookie_name = 'like_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id; my $unlike_cookie_name = 'unlike_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id; if( !$self->signed_cookie( $unlike_cookie_name ) && $client_check ){ ++$unlike_count; $self->db->update( 'posts', {'unliked' => $unlike_count}, {'id' => $article_id} ); } if( $self->signed_cookie( $like_cookie_name ) ){ --$like_count; $self->db->update( 'posts', {'liked' => $like_count}, {'id' => $article_id} ); $self->signed_cookie( $like_cookie_name => '', {expires => 1}); } # Store session for 'dislike' action where key is #'unlike_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id # and value is $title_alias.'_'.$article_id Session->voting( $self, 'unlike_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, $title_alias.'_'.$article_id ); $self->render( template => 'voting/vote', section_ident => $title_alias, id => $article_id, like_id => 'like_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, unlike_id => 'unlike_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, client_id => $user_id, unlike_btn_name => 'unlike'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, like_btn_name => 'like'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, status => $status, unliked_cnt => $unlike_count, liked_cnt => $like_count ); }#--------------- 1;

模板投票/vote.html.ep
使用它来呈现'like'和'dislike'动作

%# voting/vote.html.ep <button type="button" name="<%= $like_btn_name %>" onclick="Voting('<%= $section_ident %>', '<%= $id %>', '<%= $like_id %>', '<%= $client_id %>'); this.disabled='disabled';" id="chevron_stl"<%= $status %>> <span id="<%= $section_ident.'_'.$client_id.'-up' %>" class="glyphicon glyphicon-chevron-up" aria-hidden="true"></span> </button> <span id="<%= $like_id %>" class="like_unlike"><%= $liked_cnt %></span> <button type="button" name="<%= $unlike_btn_name %>" onclick="Voting('<%= $section_ident %>', '<%= $id %>', '<%= $unlike_id %>', '<%= $client_id %>'); this.disabled='disabled';" id="chevron_stl"<%= $status %>> <span id="<%= $section_ident.'_'.$client_id.'-down' %>" class="glyphicon glyphicon-chevron-down" aria-hidden="true"></span> </button> <span id="<%= $unlike_id %>" class="like_unlike"><%= $unliked_cnt %></span>

结论

使用文章 ID、用户 ID 和操作类型（喜欢或不喜欢）的组合来创建唯一的投票 ID。 此投票 ID 可以是签名 cookie 的密钥。

在我的项目MornCat CMS中实现的简化的喜欢/不喜欢系统https://github.com/arslonga/my_blog

阿纳布罗伊乔杜里 - Apr 25 4月25日



原文:

arslonga

Posted on Apr 29

This post describes the actual Like/Dislike mechanism in the program based on Mojolicious. Web pages style is created using the Bootstrap framework. The full version of the Mojolicious like-dislike demo can be downloaded at: https://github.com/arslonga/like-dislike

So, in the start package Vote.pm we create routers for the post with a certain ID in the section named 'first-section' (for example), and also for like and dislike:

sub startup {
my $self = shift;

my $r = $self->routes;
...
$r->any('/first-section/:id' => [ id => qr/[0-9]+/ ] )->to('post#article');
...
$r->any('/likeartcl')->to('voting#like');
$r->any('/dislikeartcl')->to('voting#dislike');
...
}

A snippet of code in the Post.pm package that describes the 'article' method:

package Vote::Controller::Post;
use Mojo::Base 'Mojolicious::Controller';
use Session;
use SessCheck;
...
#---------------------------------
sub article {
#---------------------------------
my $self = shift;
my $id = $self->stash('id');
my($nickname, $status, $client_check, $user_id, $title_alias);


eval{
$nickname = $self->session('client')->[0];
$user_id = $self->session('client')->[2]; 
};
$client_check = SessCheck->client( $self, $nickname );
if( !$client_check ){
    $status = ' disabled';
}
$title_alias = (split(/\//, $self->req->url))[1];
my $data = $self->db->select( 'posts', ['*'], {id => $id} )->hash;

$self->render( 
dat             => $data,
section_ident   => $title_alias,
id              => $id,
like_id         => 'like_'.$title_alias.'_'.$id,
unlike_id       => 'unlike_'.$title_alias.'_'.$id,
client_id       => $user_id,
unlike_btn_name => 'unlike'.$title_alias.'_'.$id,
like_btn_name   => 'like'.$title_alias.'_'.$id,
status          => $status,
liked_cnt       => $data->{liked} || 0,
unliked_cnt     => $data->{unliked} || 0
);
}#---------------
...
1;

Template article.html.ep in /templates/post

% layout 'vote';

<hr>
<a class="btn btn-info" href="/first-section" role="button">
<span class="glyphicon glyphicon-arrow-left"></span>
</a>
<hr>
<div>
    <h2><%= $dat->{title} %></h2>
    <%= $dat->{body} %>
</div>
<hr>
<div class="ld-box text-right">

%# Rendering a template that describes a block of code
%# that contains two buttons:
%# 'like' and 'dislike'

<%= include 'voting/vote' %>
</div>

<script>
//---------- Like/Dislike block ---
async function LikeArtcl(titleAlias, articleId, vote_span, user_id) {
let likedislikeBox = document.querySelector('.ld-box');

  let response = await fetch("/likeartcl?title_alias=" + 
  titleAlias + 
  '&article_id=' + 
  articleId + 
  '&vote_span=' + 
  vote_span + 
  '&user_id=' + 
  user_id);

  if (response.ok) {
    let respRendr = await response.text(); 
    likedislikeBox.innerHTML = respRendr;
  }else {
    alert("Error HTTP: " + response.status);
  }
}

async function UnlikeArtcl(titleAlias, articleId, vote_span, user_id) {
let likedislikeBox = document.querySelector('.ld-box');

  let response = await fetch("/dislikeartcl?title_alias=" + 
  titleAlias + 
  '&article_id=' + 
  articleId + 
  '&vote_span=' + 
  vote_span + 
  '&user_id=' + 
  user_id);

  if (response.ok) {
    let respRendr = await response.text(); 
    likedislikeBox.innerHTML = respRendr;
  }else {
    alert("Error HTTP: " + response.status);
  }
}
//---------- Like/Dislike block END ---
</script>

Package Session.pm:

package Session;
use Mojo::Base -base;

#---------------------------------
sub user {
#---------------------------------
my($self, $c, $login, $password, $id) = @_;

$c->session( client => [$login, $password, $id], expiration => 120);
return 1;
}#---------------

# 'voting' method of Session called in 'Voting.pm' package (see code fragment below)
#---------------------------------
sub voting {
#---------------------------------
my($self, $c, $user_vote_id, $title_alias_and_id) = @_;
$c->signed_cookie( $user_vote_id => $title_alias_and_id, {expires => time + 120});
return 1;
}#---------------

#---------------------------------
sub client_expire {
#---------------------------------
my($self, $c) = @_;
delete $c->session->{'client'};
return 1;
}#---------------
1;

Package Vote/Voting.pm
Here we render template 'voting/vote.html.ep' that describes code block with 'like' and 'dislike' buttons and 'like' and 'dislike' counts

package Vote::Voting;
use Mojo::Base 'Mojolicious::Controller';
use Mojo::Util qw(trim encode decode);
use Mojo::Cookie;
use Session;

#---------------------------------
sub like {
#---------------------------------
my $self = shift;
my($nickname, $status);
eval{
$nickname = $self->session('client')->[0];  
};
my $client_check = SessCheck->client( $self, $nickname );

my $title_alias = $self->param('title_alias');
my $article_id  = $self->param('article_id');
my $user_id     = $self->param('user_id');

$status = !$client_check ? ' disabled' : '';

my $like_count = $self->db->select( 'posts', 
                                  ['liked'], 
                                  {id => $article_id} )
                                  ->hash->{liked};
my $unlike_count = $self->db->select( 'posts', 
                                    ['unliked'], 
                                    {id => $article_id} )
                                    ->hash->{unliked};
my $like_cookie_name = 'like_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id;
my $unlike_cookie_name = 'unlike_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id;

if( !$self->signed_cookie( $like_cookie_name ) && $client_check ){
    ++$like_count;
    $self->db->update( 'posts', 
                     {'liked' => $like_count}, 
                     {'id' => $article_id} );
}

if( $self->signed_cookie( $unlike_cookie_name ) ){
    --$unlike_count;
    $self->db->update( 'posts', 
                     {'unliked' => $unlike_count}, 
                     {'id' => $article_id} );
    $self->signed_cookie( $unlike_cookie_name => '', {expires => 1});
}

# Store session for 'like' action where key is 
#'like_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id
# and value is $title_alias.'_'.$article_id
Session->voting( $self, 
                 'like_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, 
                 $title_alias.'_'.$article_id 
               );

$self->render(
template        => 'voting/vote',
section_ident   => $title_alias,
id              => $article_id,
like_id         => 'like_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id,
unlike_id       => 'unlike_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id,
client_id       => $user_id,
unlike_btn_name => 'unlike'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id,
like_btn_name   => 'like'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id,
status          => $status,
liked_cnt       => $like_count,
unliked_cnt     => $unlike_count
);
}#---------------

#---------------------------------
sub dislike {
#---------------------------------
my $self = shift;
my($nickname, $status);
eval{
$nickname = $self->session('client')->[0];  
};
my $client_check = SessCheck->client( $self, $nickname );

my $title_alias = $self->param('title_alias');
my $article_id  = $self->param('article_id');
my $user_id     = $self->param('user_id');

$status = !$client_check ? ' disabled' : '';

my $unlike_count = $self->db->select( 'posts', 
                                    ['unliked'], 
                                    {id => $article_id} )
                                    ->hash->{unliked};
my $like_count = $self->db->select( 'posts', 
                                  ['liked'], 
                                  {id => $article_id} )
                                  ->hash->{liked};
my $like_cookie_name = 'like_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id;
my $unlike_cookie_name = 'unlike_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id;

if( !$self->signed_cookie( $unlike_cookie_name ) && $client_check ){
    ++$unlike_count;
    $self->db->update( 'posts', 
                     {'unliked' => $unlike_count}, 
                     {'id' => $article_id} );
}

if( $self->signed_cookie( $like_cookie_name ) ){
    --$like_count;
    $self->db->update( 'posts', 
                     {'liked' => $like_count}, 
                     {'id' => $article_id} );
    $self->signed_cookie( $like_cookie_name => '', {expires => 1});
}

# Store session for 'dislike' action where key is 
#'unlike_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id
# and value is $title_alias.'_'.$article_id
Session->voting( $self, 
                 'unlike_user'.$user_id.'_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id, 
                 $title_alias.'_'.$article_id );

$self->render(
template        => 'voting/vote',
section_ident   => $title_alias,
id              => $article_id,
like_id         => 'like_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id,
unlike_id       => 'unlike_'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id,
client_id       => $user_id,
unlike_btn_name => 'unlike'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id,
like_btn_name   => 'like'.$title_alias.'_'.$article_id,
status          => $status,
unliked_cnt     => $unlike_count,
liked_cnt       => $like_count
);
}#---------------
1;

Template voting/vote.html.ep
Use it for both rendering 'like' and 'dislike' actions

%# voting/vote.html.ep

<button type="button" name="<%= $like_btn_name %>" 
onclick="Voting('<%= $section_ident %>', 
                   '<%= $id %>', 
                   '<%= $like_id %>', 
                   '<%= $client_id %>'); 
                   this.disabled='disabled';" 
                   id="chevron_stl"<%= $status %>>
<span id="<%= $section_ident.'_'.$client_id.'-up' %>" 
class="glyphicon glyphicon-chevron-up" aria-hidden="true"></span>
</button>
<span id="<%= $like_id %>" class="like_unlike"><%= $liked_cnt %></span>

<button type="button" name="<%= $unlike_btn_name %>" 
onclick="Voting('<%= $section_ident %>', 
                     '<%= $id %>', 
                     '<%= $unlike_id %>', 
                     '<%= $client_id %>'); 
                     this.disabled='disabled';" 
                     id="chevron_stl"<%= $status %>>
<span id="<%= $section_ident.'_'.$client_id.'-down' %>" 
class="glyphicon glyphicon-chevron-down" aria-hidden="true"></span>
</button>
<span id="<%= $unlike_id %>" class="like_unlike"><%= $unliked_cnt %></span>

Conclusion

Use the combination of article ID, user ID, and kind of action (like or dislike) to create a unique voting ID. This voting ID can be a key of signed cookie.

A simplified like/dislike system implemented in my project MornCat CMS at https://github.com/arslonga/my_blog

arnabroychowdhury - Apr 25
item.author: arslonga

item.title: 为什么我们选择 Figma 作为我们的原型设计工具 | Why we chose Figma as our prototyping tool
item.description:
作为团队中唯一的设计师，我在第一季度所做的一件事就是尝试不同的原型制作工具，以找到适合我的设计工作流程的工具，并帮助我制作开发团队和业务团队可以用来评估的原型。产品。 该团队需要可点击的原型，可以处理来自 JSON 文件、API 或 google 表格文件的动态数据，以及可以处理输入、状态和逻辑的原型。 以下是我尝试过的原型制作工具以及我在使用它们时的经验。

成帧器

我对 Framer 的第一印象是，这是未来的原型制作工具。 我很高兴使用它，尤其是当我发现您可以编写自己的交互式组件并将它们添加到组件库时。 我记得当时想，设计团队和开发团队将使用同一个组件库，哇！ 但事实并非如此，因为您不能将大多数 npm 包导入 Framer，至少现在还不能，而且我们在Bit.dev中的组件库依赖于 antd。

另一种选择是以无代码方式重新创建 UI 组件，这似乎不是我的好时间，因为我已经在 Figma 中完成了带有悬停状态和自动布局的 UI 组件，以及我使用的组件'll recreate 将没有 Framer 与其内置组件的交互性。 不要误会我的意思，Framer 为可点击原型提供了出色的动画和过渡效果，但这不是优先事项。 我们想要具有状态的原型，可以处理动态数据，可以捕获输入数据并将其传递到另一个屏幕，并且可以使用 if-then 逻辑，而 Framer 不会为我们做这些。

我认为 Framer 是一个很棒的工具，有着光明的未来，但它不是 GetPaid 团队现在可以使用的工具，也许直到他们能够支持我们组件库中使用的 npm 包。开发。

与我之前学习和使用的其他工具相比，它有点不同。 我花了一段时间才弄到它，当我终于弄到它时，我意识到它不是一个好的设计工具，添加基本的设计细节需要花费太多精力，而且你甚至不能在那里进行混合模式。 因此，结合陡峭的学习等于解决 Figma 可点击原型。

结论

我认为这些工具不适合我的工作流程，因为在大多数情况下，我必须重做我已经在 Figma 中完成的设计工作，而且大多数时候我会得到不值得的结果。 借助 Figma 的原型制作工具，我能够实现出色的可点击原型，我们将作为整个团队使用，甚至在发布活动期间向我们的用户展示其中的一些原型。 设计文件仍然很乱，有很多原型线绕来绕去，我希望 if-then 调节是 Figma 可以在不久的将来实现的东西。



原文:

The end of March marked the end of Q1 here at GetPaid Africa. As the only designer in the team, one of the things that I did during Q1 was to try different prototyping tools to find the one that will fit my design workflow and help me produce prototypes that the development team and business team could use to evaluate the product. The team wanted clickable prototypes that can handle dynamic data from a JSON file, an API, or google sheets file, and one that can handle input, states, and logic. Here are the prototyping tools that I tried and my experience while using them.

Framer

My first impression of Framer was that this is the prototyping tool of the future. I was so excited to use it especially when I found out that you can code your own interactive components and add them to a component library. I remember thinking, the design team and the development team will be using the same component library, wow! But that wasn’t the case because you can’t import most npm packages to Framer, at least not yet and our component library in Bit.dev depends on antd.

Another option will be to recreate the UI components the no-code way, and that didn’t seem like a good usage of my time since I have UI components done in Figma already with hovers states and auto layout, and also the components that I’ll recreate won’t have that interactivity that Framer has with their built-in components. And don’t get me wrong, Framer has great animations and transitions for clickable prototypes, but that’s wasn’t the priority. We wanted prototypes that have states, can handle dynamic data, can capture input data and pass it to another screen, and can use if-then logic and Framer wasn’t going to do that for us.

I think Framer is a great tool, with a bright future ahead of it but it's not a tool that can be used by the GetPaid team for now, maybe until they are able to support npm packages that are used in our components library in bit.dev.

Axure RP

Axure RP seemed promising, it had most of the things that we needed including if-then conditioning which was huge. The challenge with Axure is that I found the learning curve to be very steep. It was a bit different compared to other tools that I have learned and used before. It took me a while to get it, and when I finally did, I realized that it is not a good design tool, takes too much effort to add basic design details, and you can’t even do blending modes in there. So that combined with steep learning equaled settling for Figma clickable prototypes.

Conclusion

I think that those tools didn’t fit in my work flow because in most instances I had to redo the design work that I had done in Figma already and I would most of the times get results that weren’t worth the hustle. With Figma’s prototyping tool, I was able to implement good clickable prototypes that we would use as the whole team and even show some of them to our users during our launch event. The design file is still messy, lots of prototype wires going around and I hope that if-then conditioning is something that Figma could implement in the near future.

item.title: 如何编写解锁更多喜欢、评论和覆盖面的 Instagram 标题 | How to Write Instagram Captions that Unlock More Likes, Comments, and Reach
item.description:
这不仅是好的设计，而且写得好的 Instagram 标题对于吸引观众的眼球也同样重要。 只是为了让事情更顺利，这里有几种写出好的 Instagram 标题的方法。


第一部分：

为此，它必须包含有力的词语、引人入胜的问题或未知的、有趣的事实。 这将抓住读者注意力的最初几秒钟。

核心信息：

现在是您帖子的主要信息目的。 这包括有关帖子内容的详细信息。 在文本中换行以获得适当的间距。 它为您的读者创造了一个清晰的愿景。

订婚电话：

现在，通过让他们用表情符号或竖起大拇指发表评论，或者让他们在评论部分发表意见，轻轻地与您的观众互动。 这会让他们感觉与帖子有联系。

呼吁采取行动：

这是创建帖子的主要目的，所以千万不要错过添加号召性用语的机会。 让他们通过访问您的其他社交媒体帐户和您的网站等来了解更多关于您的信息。这是结束您的 Instagram 标题的强制性方式。

小细节在任何过程中都非常重要，其中一个需要关注的细节是数字营销中引人注目的Instagram标题。 细节可以让你从其他人中脱颖而出，这就是将为你改变游戏的东西。 因此，尝试这些操作，您将看到不同之处。

原文:

It's not just good design, but a well-written Instagram caption is also equally important to catch the eyes of your audience. Instagram is the most used platform, and driving traffic to your daily posts is a tough nut to crack. Just to make things smoother, here are a few ways to write a good Instagram caption.


The first section:

The very first few words or sentences of your Instagram captions should be compelling. For that, it must include powerful words, catchy questions, or an unknown, interesting fact. This will grab the first few seconds of your readers' attention.

Core Information:

Now comes the main informational purpose of your post. This includes details about what the post is about. Break the lines for proper spacing in the text. It creates a clean vision for your reader.

Engagement Call:

Now gently engage with your audience either by asking them to comment with an emoji or a thumbs up or by asking them to give their views in the comment section. This will make them feel connected to the post.

Call to action:

This is the main purpose of creating your post, so never miss the opportunity to add a call to action. Ask them to learn more about you by visiting your other social media accounts and your website, etc. This is the mandatory way to make your Instagram caption end.

Small details are very important in any process, and one such detail to focus upon is a compelling Instagram caption in digital marketing. Details can make you stand out from the rest, and that's what is going to change the game for you. So, try these actions and you will see the difference.

item.title: Git 备忘单 – $get git | A Git cheat sheet – $get git
item.description:
基本 Git 命令

  • 将文件添加到暂存区
  • 保存对存储库的更改并提交
git add . git commit -m " my first commit"
  • 显示你没有上演的差异
  • 两个分支的区别——branch_1 和 branch_2
 git diff branch_1 branch_2
  • 获取整个提交历史
git diff branch_1 branch_2

从暂存区撤消，但保持工作目录不变
  • 显示从工作目录中删除的文件

将所有更改合并到当前分支
  • 检查现有分支
git checkout [NameOfBranch]

获取最新版本的存储库
git pull[NameOfBranch] [url]
  • 从 repo 中获取特定的 [branch]
 git fetch [remote_url] [branch]
  • 获取分支的特定远程副本
git push [remote_url] [BrnaceName]

删除

  • 从工作索引中删除整个目录
git rem -r --cached [NameofDirecotry]
  • 删除整个目录
git rm -r -f [NameofFile]

原文:

Git Commands Comes handy

Git is a highly matured open-source software that is famous for tracking changes and collaboration among programmers. Git plays a major role in DevOPS for source code management.

The git is very efficient in speed, data integrity, and support for distributed, non-linear workflows

Basic Git commands

  • Add file to the staging area
  • Save changes to repository & commit 
git add .
git commit -m " my first commit"
  • Show difference which you are not staged
  • Differences between the two branches – branch_1 and branch_2 
git diff branch_1 branch_2
  • Get the entire commit history 
git diff branch_1 branch_2

Git Undo

  • Revoke from the staging area but leave the working directory unchanged
  • Shows which files be removed from the working directory

Git Branches 

git branch [NameOfBranch]

git branch -d [NameOfBranch]
  • Merging all changes to current branch
  • Checking an existing branch 
git checkout [NameOfBranch]

Git Tag

Git Remote repositories

  • Getting latest version of the repository 
git pull[NameOfBranch] [url]
  • Gets a specific [branch] from the repo 
git fetch [remote_url] [branch]
  • Get speficif remote copy of branch 
git push [remote_url] [BrnaceName]

Git Delete

  • Remove the entire Directory from the working index 
git rem -r --cached [NameofDirecotry]
  • Deleting an entire directory 
git rm -r -f [NameofFile]

item.title: 网络技术作为一个频谱 | Web Technology as a Spectrum
item.description:
想象一个频谱。 一方面，您拥有以模板为中心的网站建设者，例如 SquareSpace。 再往前走一点，你就有了 Webflow。 在另一端，你有原始的 HTML/CSS。

这是关于这个主题的一个非常好的观点。 还有一个我想谈的，不仅仅是在 CSS 的上下文中。

开发人员何时变得无关紧要的问题一直存在。 学生们使用廉价的所见即所得编辑器、易于设置的解决方案（如 Wordpress 和 Shopify）以及最后但并非最不重要的 GitHub Copilot 来制作网站，这让人类编码人员怀疑他们是否会很快被解雇。 答案一直是否定的，而且会持续很长时间。

好的自动化工具可以减少摩擦，并为开发人员提供解决更复杂问题的工具：

  • 它也有主题，因此后端开发人员可以构建复杂的插件而不必担心用户体验
  • 比起干净的后端，我更看重定制和专门构建的 UI。 像这样的工具采用了我不喜欢的部分（例如服务器端编程），并为我提供了开箱即用的运行良好的解决方案。 与此同时，我可以更深入地挖掘前端开发并专注于我喜欢的部分。 这样做我很快就超越了自动化解决方案，并重新开始手写我自己的代码。 正如 Josh 所认为的那样：我提升了频谱，到最后，这给了我更多的灵活性，使用更多的通用工具。

    不过，并非所有工具都能让我如此轻松地转向我想要的方向。 有时工具会产生自己的一系列问题，突然间，您需要成为干墙施工专家才能挂画。 与几年前相比，这就是我最终成为一名全栈开发人员的原因。

    自从 JavaScript 第一次在服务器上运行以来，它就发起了一场十字军东征，以吞噬越来越多的前端开发方面。 虽然 10 年前你可以通过 HTML、CSS 和一点点 jQuery 获得一份体面的工作，但如今大多数职位的基本要求是深厚的 JavaScript 知识。 随着我学习 ES6、Node 和 Vue，我逐渐失去了对设计和布局的关注，以至于我什至从未接触过 Figma（或者说 Sketch）。 当然，这些工具一直在为我解决问题，但它们无法让我提升自己的能力。 它们不像 HTML 和 CSS 那样通用。 我现在不得不关心诸如路由和数据结构之类的事情，而不是制作漂亮的网站——我认为服务器和浏览器正在为我做的事情。

    正是这些工具最终创造了 Chris Coyier 后来将其描述为The Great Divide的东西，一方面是专注于工程的前端，另一方面是专注于设计的前端。 反应式框架不仅解决了它们自己的问题，它们还创造了如此多的新问题，以至于一所全新的工程师学校紧随其后。 过时了！

    一个工具不仅可以帮助我完成当前的任务，而且可以帮助我成为一个更好的开发人员，还是它试图把我锁在一个有围墙的花园里？当前的任务，但它可以帮助我成为一个更好的开发人员，还是它试图把我锁在有围墙的花园里？



原文:

Josh W. Comeau talked on Twitter about no-code solutions, specifically for CSS, and how they impact what developers work on after trivial tasks in their workflow have been automated.

Imagine a spectrum. At one end, you have template-focused website builders like SquareSpace. A little further along, you have Webflow. At the opposite end, you have raw HTML/CSS.

This is a very nice perspective on the subject. And one I'd like to talk about, not just in the context of CSS.

The question of when developers would become irrelevant has always come up. Students making websites on the cheap, WYSIWYG editors, Easy-to-setup solutions like Wordpress and Shopify, and last but not least GitHub Copilot have human coders wondering if they'll be pushed out of a job soon. The answer has always been no and will be for a very long time.

Good automation tools reduce friction and give developers the tools to tackle more complex problems:

  • Wordpress gave me a CMS so I don't have to worry about that and can create a nice frontend
  • It also had themes, so backend developers could build sophisticated plugins and not worry about the UX
  • GitHub Copilot writes Regular Expressions and SQL that works well enough for me, and sometimes lets me second-guess my own code.

I value a custom and purpose-built UI more than a clean backend. Tools like those took the parts that I didn't like (such as server-side programming) and gave me solutions that worked well enough out of the box. In the meantime, I could dig even deeper in frontend development and specialize in the parts that I like. Doing that I quickly overtook the automated solutions and was back to hand-writing my own code. As Josh would take it: I moved up the spectrum, to the end which gave me more flexibility with more general-purpose tools.

Not all tools allow me to shift go into the direction I want to so easily, though. Sometimes tools create their own set of problems and suddenly you need to become an expert on drywall construction in order to hang a picture. That's how I wound up being more of a full-stack developer than I would have liked a few years ago.

Ever since JavaScript first ran on a server it took on a crusade to swallow more and more aspects of frontend development. While you could score a decent job with HTML, CSS, and a little jQuery on the side 10 years ago, deep JavaScript knowledge is a basic requirement for most positions nowadays. As I learned ES6, Node and Vue, I gradually lost my focus on design and layout, to the point where I have never even touched Figma (or Sketch, speaking of) yet. Those tools have been solving problems for me, sure, but they weren't able to let me move up my spectrum. They weren't as general-purpose as HTML and CSS. Instead of crafting beautiful websites, I now had to care about things like routing and data structures – things I thought the server and the browser were doing for me.

It's tools like these that eventually created what Chris Coyier would later describe as The Great Divide, with engineering-focused front enders on one side and design-focused ones on the other. Reactive frameworks have not only solved their own problem, they created so many new ones that an entire new school of engineers followed in their wake. So much for becoming obsolete!

Josh’s perspective on no-code solutions brings a nice way to evaluate tools with it. Can a tool help me not only with my current task but can it help me become a better developer, or does it try to lock me inside a walled garden?current task but can it help me become a better developer, or does it try to lock me inside a walled garden?

item.description:
索诺维信息

发表于我们将重点关注三个：感觉神经的、传导的和混合的。 感觉神经是老年人中最常见的。 例如，嘈杂的噪音、受伤、麻疹、糖尿病或高烧等病毒感染都会造成损害。 治疗通常是清晰的听力。 传导性听力损失是由于某些东西阻碍了声波正常传播，例如液体、耳垢堆积、肿瘤或耳道中的异物。 这种类型的损失通常可以通过手术修复。 混合是当你有一点前两个问题时。 可以尝试手术，但听力清晰是可能的治疗方法。

sonoviveinfo

Posted on Apr 29

SonoVive There are five types of hearing loss. We will focus on three: Sensorineural, conductive, and mixed. Sensorineural is the most common among aging adults. For examples, loud noise, injury, some viral infections like measles, diabetes, or high fever can cause damage. Treatment is usually Clear Hearing. Conductive hearing loss is due to something blocking sound waves from traveling properly, like fluid, wax buildup, tumor, or a foreign object in the ear canal. This type of loss can often be repaired surgically. Mixed is when you have a little of both of the first two problems. Surgery may be tried, but a clear hearing is the likely treatment.

item.title: 如何设置 SSH | How to Set up SSH
item.description:
丹金0213

发表于什么是 SSH

  • 如何生成 ssh-key
  • 如何配置生成的 ssh 密钥
  • 用例 1：GitHub
  • 用例 2：远程服务器
  • 参考

    • 用户可以通过用户名+密码或ssh密钥访问远程机器。 我个人更喜欢 ssh-key 身份验证而不是传统的登录身份验证，因为您不仅可以在远程计算机上使用 ssh-key 进行身份验证，还可以在 GitHub 等上进行身份验证。

    与使用传统登录凭据分配单独的用户帐户相比，使用多个 SSH 密钥向多个个人授予安全服务器访问权限具有三个优势。

    • 无需共享密码即可授予多方访问权限。
    • 简化权限管理； 各方以同一用户身份登录，从而共享权限。
    • 允许根据需要轻松撤销访问权限。

    有多种算法可以生成 ssh-key：RSA、DSA、ECDSA 和 EdDSA。 最常用的算法是 RSA（ssh-keygen 的默认选项），但 EdDSA 较新，与密钥长度相比提供了最高的安全级别。

    简单地：
    您需要使用 ssh-agent，它是一个帮助程序，用于跟踪用户的身份密钥及其密码。 确保 ssh-agent 正在运行：
    eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"

    将主机添加到“~/.ssh/config”：

     Host myRemoteServer HostName 198.222.111.33 User john IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 IdentitiesOnly yes

    最后，将您的 SSH 密钥添加到代理：

    或将所有生成的密钥添加到 SSH 代理

    ssh-add

    第一的，
    pbcopy < ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

    单击我的个人资料图片 -> 设置 -> 在侧边栏的“访问”部分中，单击 SSH 和 GPG 密钥 -> 单击新建 SSH 密钥或添加 SSH 密钥 -> 将复制的密钥粘贴到“密钥”字段中。

    首先，将公钥复制到远程服务器：
    ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub john@198.222.111.33

    进而：
    罗伯特·塔利亚维亚—— Apr 26 4月26日



    原文:

    DanKim0213

    Posted on Apr 29

    1. What is SSH
    2. Advantages of ssh-key authentication
    3. How to generate ssh-key
    4. How to configure generated ssh keys
    5. Use-case 1: GitHub
    6. Use-case 2: Remote server
    7. References

    SSH stands for Secure Shell, by which users can manage systems or transfer of files from computer to computer. Users can access to remote machines by username + password or ssh key. I personally prefer ssh-key authentication to traditional login authentication since you can use ssh-key to authenticate yourself not only on remote computers, but also on GitHub and so on.

    2. Advantages of ssh-key authentication

    The use of multiple SSH keys to grant secure server access to multiple individuals affords three advantages over assigning separate user accounts with traditional login credentials.

    • Grants access to multiple parties without sharing passwords.
    • Simplifies permission management; all parties log in as the same user and thereby share permissions.
    • Allow for easy access revocation as needed.

    3. How to generate ssh-key

    There are multiple algorithms to generate ssh-key: RSA, DSA, ECDSA, and EdDSA. Mostly used algorithm is RSA (the default option for ssh-keygen), but EdDSA is newer and provides the highest security level compared to key length.

    simply:
    ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "john@example.com" or more securely:

    ssh-keygen -o -a 100 -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 -C "john@example.com"

    • -t specifies the type of key to create, in our case the Ed25519.
    • -f specifies the filename of the generated key file.
    • -C specifies a comment.

    4. How to configure generated ssh keys

    You need to use ssh-agent, which is a helper program that keeps track of user's identity keys and their passphrases. Make sure ssh-agent is running:
    eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"

    Add a host onto "~/.ssh/config":

    Host myRemoteServer
  HostName 198.222.111.33
  User john
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
  IdentitiesOnly yes

    Finally, add your SSH key to the agent:

    option1 (Mac user): If you're using macOS Sierra 10.12.2 or later, you will need to modify your ~/.ssh/config file to automatically load keys into the ssh-agent and store passphrases in your keychain.

    Host *
  AddKeysToAgent yes
  UseKeychain yes
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

    ssh-add -K ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

    option2 (linux user): And then, add your newly generated Ed25519 key to SSH agent

    ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

    or add all generated keys to SSH agent

    ssh-add

    5. Use-case 1: GitHub

    First,
    pbcopy < ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

    Click my profile image -> Settings -> In the "Access" section of the sidebar, click SSH and GPG keys -> Click New SSH key or Add SSH key -> paste the copied key into "Key" field.

    6. Use-case 2: Remote server

    First of all, copy the public key to the remote server:
    ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub john@198.222.111.33

    And then:
    ssh john@198.222.111.33 or:

    ssh myRemoteServer

    7. References

    Robert Tagliavia - Apr 26
item.author: dankim0213

item.title: Opengl、Vulkan 和 3D 中的浮点精度 - 第 2 部分 | Floating Point Precision in Opengl, Vulkan and 3D in General – Part 2
item.description:
我们在之前的文章中已经看到为什么浮点表示可能会在场景中晃动我们的对象。 我们现在将看到如何使用空间变化技巧来缓解这个问题。

关于世界、模型和视图空间的简要提醒

在处理 3D 坐标时，我们一般使用 3 个坐标系：世界空间、模型空间和视图空间。 世界空间卡在地上。 模型空间卡在对象上。 所以你的场景中有很多模型空间，每个对象一个。 视野空间卡在相机上。 在本文中，我不会谈论定义相机镜头的投影空间。

所以在这里，P 相对于模型空间的坐标是 (1, 0, 1)。 但我们也可以定义 P 相对于其他空间的坐标：

  • 这些坐标的精度如何？

    可以看出，P_world 比 P_model 有更高的量级，因为 P 远离 World 空间原点。 好吧，在这个例子中不多，但想象一下模型空间距离世界空间数百万英里。 考虑到浮动精度误差会发生什么？

    但是如果我们使用 P_model 来定位 P 会发生什么呢？

    如此接近以至于在相对于模型空间移动它时几乎看不到 P 跳跃。 但是，您可能已经注意到模型空间必须与世界空间可表示值对齐。 在这种情况下，移动模型空间会使其从一个位置跳到另一个位置。 所以要使用这个技巧，你必须在你的兴趣点附近定义一个固定的空间，并相对于这个参考空间移动你的点。

    视图空间呢？

    进行 3D 渲染时，视图空间会移动很多。 它可能是最具活力的空间。 它可以连接到赛车游戏的汽车、FPS 的播放器、CAD 应用程序的鼠标等等……所以如果我们不想让我们的用户感到头疼，我们真的必须确保这个空间顺利移动。 为此，我们可以使用与以前相同的技巧。 我们可以定义一个“三脚架”空间，而不是相对于世界空间定位相机，并相对于这个三脚架定位你的相机：

    不是带有额外步骤的双精度吗？

    最后，我们所做的是用 2 个浮点数对位置进行编码，从而为我们提供 64 位浮点精度。 但是这个技巧可以使用你当前的渲染引擎架构来实现。 它不需要在计算中使用双精度值，并且任何渲染引擎都已经有一个可以用来执行此操作的“翻译”对象。 最后但同样重要的是，它不会改变您当前的浮动精度 API。

    不幸的是，由于各种原因，渲染引擎需要在某个时间点表达视图空间中的所有坐标。 像我们所做的那样累积变换并不能解决我们在视图空间中表达坐标时会遇到的精度误差。 我们将在下一篇文章中看到如何通过对矩阵累加代码进行非常局部的修改来处理这个问题。



原文:

We have seen in a previous article why floating point representation may shake our objects in scene. We will now see how to use space change tricks to mitigate this problem.

A brief reminder about World, Model and View space

When dealing with 3D coordinates, we generally use 3 coordinate systems: World space, Model space and View space. World space is stuck on the ground. Model space is stuck on the object. So there are lots of model spaces in you scene, one for each object. View space is stuck to the camera. I won’t talk about Projection space, which define camera lens, in this article.

Coordinates of P are always relative to a space. So here, coordinates of P relative to Model space are (1, 0, 1). But we can Also define coordinates of P relative to other spaces:

  • P_model = (1, 0, 1)
  • P_view = (-1, 0, 3)
  • P_world = (4, 0, 5)

What about precision of those coordinates ?

One can see that P_world have a higher magnitude than P_model, because P is far away from World space origin. Well, not so much in this exemple, but imagine Model spaces millions miles away from World Space. What happen when taking in account floating precision error ?

P will actually jump to a floating representable position. But what happen if we locate P using P_model instead ?

P would jump to a much closer position. So close that you would barely see P jumping when moving it relative to model space. However, you may have noticed that model space has to be aligned into world space representable values. In this case, moving model space will make it jump from one position to another. So to use this trick, you have to define a fixed space close to your point of interest, and move you points relative to this reference space.

What about View space ?

The View space moves a LOT when doing 3D rendering. It is probably the most dynamic space. It can be linked to a car for racing game, player for FPS, mouse for CAD application, etc… So we really have to ensure this space move smoothly if we don’t want to give our users a headache. To do so, we can use the same trick than before. Instead of locating camera relative to world space, we can define a “Tripod” space, and locate your camera relative to this tripod:

Isn’t it Double precision with extra steps ?

In the end, what we did was encoding a position with 2 floats, giving us 64bit floating precision. But this trick can be implemented using your current render engine architecture. It doesn’t require using double precision values in computations, and any render engine already has a “Translation” object that can be used to do this. Last but not least, it doesn’t change your current floating precision API.

Unfortunately, for various reasons, Render engines require expressing all the coordinates in View space at some point. Accumulating transformations like we did won’t solve the precision error we will have when expressing coordinates in View space. We will see in the next article how to handle this with very local modifications to our matrix accumulation code.
item.author: tandrieu

item.title: 电子商务的最佳运输 API | Best Shipping API for eCommerce
item.description:
如果您是电子商务企业，那么您就会知道运输的重要性。 尽可能快速、轻松地将您的产品提供给您的客户是重中之重。 这就是为什么找到满足您需求的最佳运输 API 很重要的原因。 这篇博文将概述可用的最佳运输 API 以及它们如何帮助您的业务发展。 我们还将讨论每个 API 的一些关键特性。 因此，如果您正在寻找新的运输解决方案，请务必继续阅读。

如果没有运输 API，企业将被迫使用缓慢且昂贵的运输方式，最终导致销售额下降和客户满意度下降。 运输 API 为电子商务企业提供了完美的解决方案，提供快速、可靠且价格合理的运输选项，可以随您的业务扩展。

这提高了透明度，并有助于在企业与其客户之间建立信任。 最终，运输 API 对企业和客户都至关重要，并且是电子商务领域不可或缺的一部分。

以下是电子商务的最佳运输 API 列表：

这对在线零售商来说是个好消息，因为它应该让包裹递送变得更简单、更快捷。 此外，任何想要使用它的人现在都可以使用 FedEx API，因此如果您正在寻找一种更有效的方式来处理您的运输需求，请务必查看这个新工具。

这将为使用 UPS 系统的企业主节省时间和金钱。 如果您是电子商务企业主，请务必查看这个新 API，看看它如何帮助简化您的运输流程。

它提供了一种简单的方法来获取您的包裹的运费和打印标签，所有这些都来自您自己的网站或购物车。

这将为企业和 DHL 节省时间和金钱。

使用 EasyPost API，您可以管理库存、打印标签、跟踪包裹等。

它易于使用，可以为您节省运输成本的时间和金钱。

如果您正在寻找一种将印刷产品添加到在线商店的方法，那么 Gooten API 可能正是您所需要的。 另外，没有最低要求或安装费。 您可以通过在 Gooten 注册一个免费帐户，立即开始销售印刷产品。

通过自动化预订流程和提供实时费率，您可以让您的客户轻松快捷地运送他们的订单。

我们已经了解了如何使用 API 来减少运输时间、自动化订单处理以及为您的客户提供实时跟踪信息。

通过使用 Flexport API，企业可以节省运输成本的时间和金钱。 如果您有兴趣了解有关 Flexport API 如何帮助您的业务的更多信息，请查看他们的网站以获取更多信息。

为什么为电子商务运输 API？

电子商务格局不断发展，新技术不断涌现。 最新和重要的发展之一是航运 ​​API 的兴起。

运输 API 为电子商务企业提供了多项优势，包括：

  • 提高运输选项的灵活性和控制力

  • 为客户提供更多运输选择的能力

  • 更快的运输时间

  • 更准确的运费估算

总体而言，运输 API 为客户提供了更加简化和高效的运输体验，从而增加了电子商务企业的销售额和利润。 如果您还没有使用 Shipping API，那么值得考虑一下它如何使您的业务受益。

我们概述了电子商务的最佳运输 API，并在决定时为您提供了一些关键考虑因素。 有了正确的运输 API，您就可以专注于发展业务和开拓新市场。 那么我要选择哪个电子商务的最佳运输 API ？



原文:

If you're an ecommerce business, then you know the importance of shipping. Getting your products to your customers as quickly and easily as possible is a top priority. That's why it's important to find the best shipping API for your needs. This blog post will outline the best shipping APIs available and how they can help your business grow. We'll also discuss some of the key features of each API. So if you're looking for a new shipping solution, be sure to read on.

The Shipping API is a vital part of the eCommerce ecosystem, providing the means for businesses to get their products shipped to customers quickly and efficiently. Without a shipping API, businesses would be forced to use slow and expensive shipping methods, ultimately leading to lower sales and less customer satisfaction. A shipping API provides the perfect solution for eCommerce businesses, offering fast, reliable, and affordable shipping options that can scale with your business.

The Shipping API is also important for customers, as it allows them to track their orders and receive updates on estimated delivery times. This increases transparency and helps to build trust between businesses and their customers. Ultimately, the Shipping API is essential for both businesses and customers alike and is an integral part of the eCommerce landscape.

Here's the list of top best shipping APIs for eCommerce:

**1) FedEx API
**FedEx has just released a new API to make it easier for eCommerce businesses to connect with the shipping company. This is great news for online retailers, as it should make it simpler and faster to get packages delivered. In addition, the FedEx API is now available to anyone who wants to use it, so if you're looking for a more efficient way to handle your shipping needs, be sure to check out this new tool.

**2) UPS API
**UPS has created an API to help ecommerce businesses automate their shipping process. This will save time and money for business owners who use the UPS system. If you are an ecommerce business owner, be sure to check out this new API and see how it can help streamline your shipping process.

**3) USPS API
**The USPS API for eCommerce could be a great solution for your business. It provides an easy way to get shipping rates and print labels for your packages, all from within your own website or shopping cart.

**4) DHL API
**DHL has created an API to make it easier for eCommerce businesses to connect with them and automate their shipping processes. This will save time and money for both the business and DHL.

**5) EasyPost API
**The EasyPost API is perfect for eCommerce businesses that want to streamline their shipping and order fulfillment process. With the EasyPost API, you can manage your inventory, print labels, track packages, etc.

**6) Shippo API
**The Shippo API can streamline the shipping process for eCommerce businesses of all sizes. It's easy to use and can save you time and money on shipping costs.

**7) Gooten API
**Gooten is a print-on-demand company with an API to help eCommerce businesses sell products. If you are looking for a way to add printed products to your online store, the Gooten API may be just what you need. The Gooten API makes it easy to order prints, shirts, and other items with just a few lines of code. Plus, there are no minimums or setup fees required. You can start selling printed products today by signing up for a free account with Gooten.

**8) Uship API
**The Uship API for eCommerce can help you streamline the shipping process for your customers. By automating the booking process and providing real-time rates, you can make it easy for your customers to get their orders shipped quickly and easily.

**9) Easyship API
**The Easyship API is a powerful tool that can help you streamline your eCommerce operations and improve your customer experience. We've seen how the API can be used to reduce shipping times, automate order processing, and provide real-time tracking information for your customers.

**10) Flexport API
**Flexport's API allows eCommerce businesses to automate their shipping and order management processes. By using the Flexport API, businesses can save time and money on shipping costs. If you're interested in learning more about how the Flexport API can help your business, check out their website for more information.

Why Shipping API for eCommerce?

The eCommerce landscape is constantly evolving, and new technologies are emerging. One of the most recent and significant developments has been the rise of Shipping APIs.

Shipping APIs offer several advantages for eCommerce businesses, including:

  • Increased flexibility and control over shipping options

  • The ability to offer customers more shipping choices

  • Faster shipping times

  • More accurate shipping estimates

Overall, Shipping APIs provide a more streamlined and efficient shipping experience for customers, resulting in increased sales and profits for eCommerce businesses. If you're not yet using a Shipping API, it's worth considering how it could benefit your business.

**Conclusion:
**If you are looking for a shipping API to help power online store, consider a few options. We've outlined the best shipping APIs for eCommerce and given you some key considerations when deciding. With the right shipping API in place, you can focus on growing your business and expanding into new markets. So which Best Shipping API for eCommerce I are you going to choose?
